# Zoom Connector - Full Documentation

# Zoom Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Zoom (Zoom Connector) enables you to access the Zoom API.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, refer to the Zoom Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Zoom Connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Zoom target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

## Connection Types

Zoom connections use the following authentication types:

- Client Credentials Config
  - Client Credentials Connection Provider

- Zoom Connector Configuration
  - Bearer Connection Provider
  - Oauth Connection Provider

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Zoom Connector Reference](zoom-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# Zoom Connector 1.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] This topic is large and might take longer than usual to load in your browser. Allow extra
> time for the content to render or switch to another browser.

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

## Configurations

---

### Client Credentials Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ClientCredentialsConfig_ClientCredentials">Client Credentials Connection Provider</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Client Credentials Connection Provider

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of outbound connections to keep open at the same time.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Uri</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant gets its own.</p></td><td><p><code>https://api.zoom.us/v2</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client ID registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client secret registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://api.zoom.us/oauth/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes requested during the dance. If not provided, it defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store used to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Oauth Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

Each instance or tenant gets its own.</p></td><td><p><code>https://api.zoom.us/v2</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumerKey registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumerSecret registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Service providerâs authorization endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://www.zoom.com/oauth/authorize</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Service providerâs accessToken endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://www.zoom.com/oauth/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes requested during the dance. If not provided, it defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The resourceOwnerId which each component should use if it doesnât reference otherwise.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute right after receiving an accessToken.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to an &lt;http:listener-config /&gt; used to create the listener that catches the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store used to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default one.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete A Chatbot Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-achatbot-message>`

Deletes a message sent by your Chatbot app. To get authorized, make a POST request to the
`/oauth/token` endpoint with the `client_credentials` grant type. Use
`https://api.zoom.us/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials` as the endpoint for the request. You
must send your clientID and secret as a basic base64 encoded authorization header, such as
`Basic base64Encode({client_id}:{client_sceret})`. Next, use the token received (access_token) as a
bearer token while making the DELETE /im/chat/messages/{message_id} request to delete a message.
Learn more about how to authorize chatbots in the
[Chatbot Authorization](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/team-chat/installation-and-authentication/)
guide. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /im/chat/messages/{message_id} endpoint.

- Scope: `imchat:bot`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`
- Authorization Flow: Client Credentials Flow

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Edit Chatbot Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:edit-chatbot-message>`

Edits a message that was
[sent](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/chatbot/#tag/chatbot-messages/POST/im/chat/messages) by
your Chatbot app. After sending a message using the Send Chatbot Message API, you must store the
messageId returned in the response so that you can make edits to the associated message using this
API. To get authorized, make a POST request to the `/oauth/token` endpoint with the
`client_credentials` grant type. Use `https://api.zoom.us/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials`
as the endpoint for the request. You must send your clientID and secret as a basic base64 encoded
authorization header, such as `Basic base64Encode({client_id}:{client_sceret})`. Next, use the token
received (access_token) as a bearer token while making the PUT /im/chat/messages/{message_id}
request to edit a chatbot message. Learn more about how to authorize chatbots in the
[Chatbot Authorization](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/team-chat/installation-and-authentication/)
guide. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /im/chat/messages/{message_id} endpoint.

- Scope: `imchat:bot`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`
- Authorization Flow: Client Credentials Flow

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the message that needs to be updated. ## Sendchatbot

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:sendchatbot>`

Send chatbot messages from your marketplace Chatbot app. To get authorized, make a POST request to
the `/oauth/token` endpoint with the `client_credentials` grant type. Use
`https://api.zoom.us/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials` as the endpoint for the request. You
will need to send your clientID and secret as a basic base64 encoded authorization header, such as
`Basic base64Encode({client_id}:{client_sceret})`. Next, use the token received (access_token) as a
bearer token while making the POST /im/chat/messages request to send chatbot messages. Learn more
about how to authorize chatbots in the
[Chatbot Authorization](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/team-chat/installation-and-authentication/)
guide. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /im/chat/messages endpoint.

- Scope: `imchat:bot`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`
- Authorization Flow: Client Credentials Flow

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Unauthorize

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that it is impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the resource owner whose access should be invalidated.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Zoom Connector Configuration](#ZoomConfig)

## Account

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account>`

Gets details of a sub account that is listed under a master account. Your account must be a master
account to retrieve sub accounts' details. Zoom allows only
[approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use master APIs and create sub
accounts. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us** for more details. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts/{accountId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled.
- Scope: `account:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Account Billing

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-billing>`

Gets
[billing information](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060580) of
a sub account. Only master accounts can use this API. Zoom allows only
[approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use master APIs and manage sub
accounts' billing information. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us** for
more details. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts/{accountId}/billing
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled.
- Scope: `billing:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## Account Billing Invoices

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-billing-invoices>`

Lists [invoices](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064306) of a
Zoom account. To list a regular Zoom accountâs invoices or a master accountâs invoices, provide `me`
as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. To list a sub accountâs invoices, provide the
account ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. You can make one API request per
account (`accountId`) every five minutes until the daily limit is reached. This API has a daily
limit of six requests per account (`accountId`). This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/accounts/{accountId}/billing/invoices endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account must be enrolled in Pro or a higher plan.
- Scope: `billing:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The account ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date for the invoice query in <code>yyyy-mm-dd</code> format. The date range between <code>from</code> and <code>to</code> must not exceed one year. The range defined must fall within the past three years.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date for the invoice query in <code>yyyy-mm-dd</code> format.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Account Billing Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-billing-update>`

Updates
[billing information](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060580) of
a sub account. This API can only be used by master accounts that pay all billing charges of their
associated sub accounts. Zoom allows only
[approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use master APIs and manage sub
accounts' billing information. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us** for
more details. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /accounts/{accountId}/billing
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled.
- Scope: `billing:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

## Account Call Logs

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-call-logs>`

Use this API to return an accountâs
[call logs](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069950). This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/call_logs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date from which you would like to get the call logs. The start date must be within the past six months. The API only returns data pertaining to a month. Thus, the date range (defined using <code>from</code> and <code>to</code> fields) for which the call logs are returned must not exceed a month.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The end date up to which you would like to get the call logs for. The end date must be within the past six months.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of the call logs. The value can be either <code>all</code> or <code>missed</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter the API response by <a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069950">path</a> of the call. ## Account Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-create>`

Creates a sub account under a master account. Your account must be a master account to create sub
accounts. Zoom allows only [approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use
master APIs and manage sub accounts. Email the partner programs team at
[partner-success@zoom.us](mailto:partner-success@zoom.us) for more details. The created account user
will receive a confirmation email. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /accounts
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled.
- Scope: `account:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Account Disassociate

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-disassociate>`

Disassociate a sub account from its master account. This will leave the sub account intact but it
will no longer be associated with the master account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to
the /accounts/{accountId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled. The account making
  this API request must be a [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/).
- Scope: `account:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Account Managed Domain

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-managed-domain>`

Get a sub accountâs
[managed domains](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066259). This
API can be used by Zoom Accounts that are on a Pro or a higher plan as well accounts that have
master and sub accounts options enabled. To get managed domains of the master account, provide `me`
as the value for accountId in the path parameter. Provide the sub accountâs Account ID as the value
of accountId path parameter to get managed domains of the sub account. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /accounts/{accountId}/managed_domains endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled.
- Scope: `account:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Account Options Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-options-update>`

Update a sub accountâs options under the master account. Your account must be a master account to
update the options for sub accounts. Zoom only assigns this privilege to trusted partners. This
operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /accounts/{accountId}/options endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled. The account making
  this API request must be a [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/).
- Scope: `account:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Account Plan Addon Cancel

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-plan-addon-cancel>`

[Cancel additional plan](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066687)
of a sub account. The cancellation does not provide a refund for the current subscription. The
service remains active for the current session. This API can only be used by master accounts that
pay all billing charges of their associated Pro or higher sub accounts. Zoom allows only
[approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use master APIs and manage sub
accounts' subscriptions. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us** for more
details. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /accounts/{accountId}/plans/addons/status
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with master account option enabled. The sub account must be a
  paid account.
- Scope: `billing:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

## Account Plan Addon Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-plan-addon-create>`

Subscribe a sub account to a Zoom add-on plan. This API can only be used by master accounts that pay
all billing charges of their associated Pro or higher subaccounts. Zoom allows only approved
partners to use [master APIs](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) and manage subaccounts'
subscriptions. For more information, email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us**.
This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /accounts/{accountId}/plans/addons endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with master account option enabled. The subaccount must be a
  paid account whose billing charges are paid by its master account.
- Scopes: `billing:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The account ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Account Plan Addon Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-plan-addon-update>`

Update an additional plan for a sub account. This API can only be used by master accounts that pay
all billing charges of their associated Pro or higher sub accounts. Zoom allows only
[approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use master APIs and manage sub
accounts' subscriptions. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us** for more
details. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /accounts/{accountId}/plans/addons
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with master account enabled. The sub account must be a paid
  account. The billing charges for the sub account must be paid by the master account.
- Scopes: `billing:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

for a sub account. This API can only be used by master accounts that pay all billing charges of
their associated Pro or higher sub accounts. Zoom allows only

accounts' subscriptions. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us** for more

manage sub accounts' subscriptions. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us**

## Account Plans

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-plans>`

Get plan information of a sub account that is managed by a master account. This API can only be used
by master accounts that pay all billing charges of their associated Pro or higher sub accounts. Zoom
allows only [approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use master APIs and
manage sub accounts' billing information. Email the partner programs team at
**partner-success@zoom.us** for more details. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/accounts/{accountId}/plans endpoint.

- Scopes: `billing:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## Account Settings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:account-settings>`

Use this API to get an accountâs settings information. To get the settings of a master account, use
`me` as the value for the `accountId` path parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /accounts/{accountId}/settings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account must be a paid account.
- Scopes: `account:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## Account Settings Update

master account, use `me` as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. This operation makes an
HTTP PATCH request to the /accounts/{accountId}/settings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The sub account must be a paid account.
- Scopes: `account:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Accounts

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:accounts>`

List all the sub accounts that have been created by a master account. Zoom allows only
[approved partners](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) to use master APIs and manage sub
accounts. Email the partner programs team at **partner-success@zoom.us** for more details. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account with master account option enabled.
- Scope: `account:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Add Anumber To Blocked List

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-anumber-to-blocked-list>`

A Zoom account owner or a user with admin privilege can block phone numbers for phone users in an
account. Blocked numbers can be inbound (numbers will be blocked from calling in) and outbound
(phone users in your account wonât be able to dial those numbers). Blocked callers will hear a
generic message stating that the person they are calling is not available. Use this API to create a
blocked list and add a number to that blocked list. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/phone/blocked_list endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account plan with Zoom phone license.
- Scope: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Add Auto Receptionist

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-auto-receptionist>`

Auto receptionists answer calls with a personalized recording and routes calls to a phone user, call
queue, common area phone, voicemail or an IVR system. Use this API to add an
[auto receptionist](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061421) to a
Zoom Phone. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /phone/auto_receptionists endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`

The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add Batch Registrants

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-batch-registrants>`

Register up to 30 registrants at once for a meeting that requires
[registration](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026). This
operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/batch_registrants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The meeting host must be a licensed user. The meeting must require registration and
  should be of type `2`, which means they should be scheduled meetings. Instant meetings and
  recurring meetings are not supported by this API.
- Scope: `meeting:write`, `meeting:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the meeting (Meeting Number).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add Batch Webinar Registrants

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-batch-webinar-registrants>`

Use this API to register up to 30 registrants at once for a scheduled webinar that requires
[registration](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061631). This
operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/batch_registrants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The webinar host must be a Licensed user. The webinar should be of type `5`, which
  means it should be a scheduled webinar. Other types of webinars are not supported by this API.
- Scope: `webinar:write`, `webinar:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

## Add Byoc Number

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-byocnumber>`

Use this API to add BYOC (Bring Your Own Carrier) phone numbers to Zoom Phone. This operation makes
an HTTP POST request to the /phone/byoc_numbers endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise plan. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`, `phone:write`, or `phone:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Add Callout Countries

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-callout-countries>`

Specify the list of
[call-out](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061683) countries for
a master account or a sub account. To add call-out enabled countries to a sub account, provide the
account ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. To add call-out enabled countries
to a master account, provide `me` as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. This operation
makes an HTTP POST request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/callout_countries endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account making this API request must be a
  [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) with SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:master`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. To add Call-out enabled countries to a sub account, provide the account ID of the sub account in the <code>accountId</code> path parameter. To add Call-out enabled countries to a master account, provide <code>me</code> as the value of the <code>accountId</code> path parameter.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add Common Area Phone

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-common-area-phone>`

Use this API to
[add a common area phone](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066552).
A common area phone can be provisioned by a Zoom account owner or a Zoom admin so that anyone in an
organization can use it, for example, if your office has shared desks that donât belong to a
specific employees, you could add a common area phone so that any person can use it. This operation
makes an HTTP POST request to the /phone/common_area_phones endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions. A
  [supported device](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060242).
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Add Internal Numbers

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-internal-numbers>`

This API allows a master account with a SIP Connected Audio plan to assign internal phone numbers,
such as for numbers that are not provided by Zoom but are owned by the organization consuming the
API, to a master account or a sub account. To add internal numbers to a sub account, provide the
account ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. To add internal numbers to a master
account, provide `me` as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. This operation makes an HTTP
POST request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/internal_numbers endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account making this API request must be a
  [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) with SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:master`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. To add internal numbers to a sub account, provide the account ID of the sub account in the <code>accountId</code> path parameter. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Add Members To Call Queue

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-members-to-call-queue>`

Add phone users and/or
[common area phones](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066552) as
members to a specific Call Queue. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/phone/call_queues/{callQueueId}/members endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account plan. Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Call Queue Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the call queue.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add Members To Shared Line Group

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-members-to-shared-line-group>`

A [shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509)
allows Zoom Phone admins to share a phone number and extension with a group of phone users or common
area phones. This gives members of the shared line group access to the groupâs direct phone number
and voicemail. Use this API to
[add members](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509-Setting-up-shared-line-groups#h_7cb42370-48f6-4a8f-84f4-c6eee4d9f0ca)
to a Shared Line Group. Note that a member can only be added to one shared line group. This
operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /phone/shared_line_groups/{sharedLineGroupId}/members
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license. A valid Shared Line Group. Account
  owner or admin privileges.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Add Phone Device

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-phone-device>`

By default, all Zoom Phone users can make and receive calls using the Zoom desktop and mobile
applications. Additionally, if a desk phone is required, use this API to
[add a desk phone and assign it](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061431)
to a user. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /phone/devices endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions. A
  [supported device](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060242).
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Add Role Members

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-role-members>`

User [roles](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983) can have a
set of permissions that allows access only to the pages a user needs to view or edit. Use this API
to
[assign a role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#h_748b6fd8-5057-4cf4-bbfd-787909c09db0)
to members. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /roles/{roleId}/members endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Pro or a higher plan.
- Scopes: `role:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## Add Setting Template

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-setting-template>`

Use this API to create a Zoom Phone setting template for an account. After creating a phone
template, the defined settings will become the default settings for an account. This operation makes
an HTTP POST request to the /phone/setting_templates endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or enterprise Zoom account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`, `phone:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add User Setting

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:add-user-setting>`

Use this API to define the voicemail access permissions of a user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Phone users can access
[shared voicemail inboxes](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069406)
in the Zoom desktop client, web portal, or provisioned desk phone. To view these settings in the
Zoom web portal, navigate to the **Admin Phone System Management and Users Rooms interface. Click
the Users tab and select \*User Settings**. Scroll down to **Voicemail Call Recordings**. This
operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /phone/users/{userId}/settings/{settingType} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Setting Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Corresponds to the setting item you wish to modify. Allowed values: <code>voice_mail</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Assign Calling Plan

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:assign-calling-plan>`

Use this API to assign a
[calling plan](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/references/billing-plans/#zoom-phone-calling-plans)
to a
[Zoom phone](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/category?id=kb_category&kb_category=5b8432168720391089a37408dabb35b0)
user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/phone/users/{userId}/calling_plans endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:write`, `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Assign Calling Plans To Common Area Phone

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:assign-calling-plans-to-common-area-phone>`

Assign calling plans to common area phone. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/phone/common_area_phones/{commonAreaPhoneId}/calling_plans endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions.
- Scope: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`
