- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Dashboard Webinar Participant Qos

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:dashboard-webinar-participant-qos>`

Retrieve details on the quality of service that participants from live or past webinars received.
This data indicates the connection quality for sending or receiving video, audio, and shared
content. If nothing is being sent or received at that time, no information will be shown in the
fields. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/metrics/webinars/{webinarId}/participants/{participantId}/qos endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, Education, or API Plan with Zoom Rooms set up.
- Scopes: `dashboard_webinars:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID or the webinar UUID. If a webinar ID is provided in the request instead of a UUID, the response will be for the latest webinar instance. If a UUID starts with "/" or contains "//" (example: "/ajXp112QmuoKj4854875==\"), you must double encode the UUID before making an API request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Participant Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Participant ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PAST</p></li><li><p>LIVE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar type.</p></td><td><p>LIVE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Dashboard Webinars

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:dashboard-webinars>`

List all the live or past webinars from a specified period of time. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /metrics/webinars endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, Education, or API Plan with Webinar add-on.
- Scopes: `dashboard_webinars:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Resource-intensive`

## Dashboard Zoom Room

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:dashboard-zoom-room>`

The Zoom Rooms dashboard metrics lets you know the type of configuration a Zoom room has and details
on the meetings held in that room. Use this API to retrieve information on a specific room. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /metrics/zoomrooms/{zoomroomId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, Education, or API Plan with Zoom Rooms set up.
- Scopes: `dashboard_zr:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Del Group Vb

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:del-group-vb>`

Delete existing virtual background file(s) from an account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE
request to the /groups/{groupId}/settings/virtual_backgrounds endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Virtual background feature must be
  [enabled](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060387) on the
  account.

- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Del User Vb

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:del-user-vb>`

Delete existing virtual background file(s) of a user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/users/{userId}/settings/virtual_backgrounds endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Virtual background feature must be
  [enabled](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060387) on the
  account.

## Delete A Blocked List

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-ablocked-list>`

A Zoom account owner or a user with admin privilege can block phone numbers for phone users in an
account. Blocked numbers can be inbound (numbers will be blocked from calling in) and outbound
(phone users in your account wonât be able to dial those numbers). Use this API to delete a blocked
list and therefore removing the associated number from the blocked list. The number will be
unblocked after the deletion. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/blocked_list/{blockedListId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account plan with Zoom phone license.
- Scope: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocked List Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the blocked list. This can be retrieved from the List Blocked List API.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete A Call Queue

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-acall-queue>`

Call queues allow you to route incoming calls to a group of users. For instance, you can use call
queues to route calls to various departments in your organization such as sales, engineering,
billing, customer service, etc. Use this API to delete a Call Queue. This operation makes an HTTP
DELETE request to the /phone/call_queues/{callQueueId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro, Business, or Education account. Account owner or admin permissions. Zoom Phone
  license.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Delete All Sip Numbers

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-all-sip-numbers>`

With SIP-connected audio, Zoom establishes a SIP trunk (a network connection specifically designed
to make and deliver phone calls) over a direct and private connection between the customerâs network
and the Zoom cloud. Meeting participants that dial into a meeting or have the meeting call them, and
are On-Net from the perspective of the customers' IP telephony network, will be connected over this
trunk rather than over the PSTN. Use this API to delete all internal numbers assigned to a sub
account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/numbers
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with SIP Connected Audio plan enabled. The account must be
  a master account.
- Scopes: `sip_trunk:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Account ID of the sub account from which the numbers are to be deleted. ## Delete A Member Slg

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-amember-slg>`

Members of the
[shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509) have
access to the groupâs phone number and voicemail. Use this API to unassign a specific member from a
Shared Line Group. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/shared_line_groups/{sharedLineGroupId}/members/{memberId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license. A valid Shared Line Group. Account
  owner or admin privileges.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared Line Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the shared line group from which you would like to remove a member.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Member Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the member who is to be removed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete A Phone Number Slg

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-aphone-number-slg>`

Use this API to unassign a specific phone number that was assigned to the
[shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509-Setting-up-shared-line-groups).
This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/shared_line_groups/{sharedLineGroupId}/phone_numbers/{phoneNumberId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license. A valid Shared Line Group. Account
  owner or admin privileges.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Delete A Shared Line Group

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-ashared-line-group>`

A [shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509)
allows Zoom Phone admins to share a phone number and extension with a group of phone users or common
area phones. Use this API to delete a Shared Line Group. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request
to the /phone/shared_line_groups/{sharedLineGroupId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin privileges.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared Line Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the shared line group that you would like to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. ## Delete Call Log

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-call-log>`

Use this API to delete a userâs
[call logs](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069950). For
user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/users/{userId}/call_logs/{callLogId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: User must belong to a Business or Enterprise account. User must have a Zoom Phone
  license.
- Scopes: `phone:write`, `phone:write:admin`, `phone_call_log:write`, `phone_call_log:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID or email address of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Call Log Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the call log. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Channel

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-channel>`

Use this API to delete a specific channel. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Zoom chat
[channels](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548) allow users to
communicate via chat in private or public groups. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_channel:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note that this API only supports a user-managed
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of a channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Delete Chat Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-chat-message>`

Delete a chat message that you previously sent to a contact or a channel. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. In the query parameter, you must provide either of the following: \*
`to_contact`: The email address of the contact to whom you sent the message. Use this parameter to
delete a message sent to an individual contact in Zoom. \* `to_channel`: The channel ID of the
channel where you sent the message. Use this parameter to delete a message sent to a channel in
Zoom. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /chat/users/{userId}/messages/{messageId}
endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_message:write`, `chat_message:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: For an account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this
API can only be used on behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has the Edit permission for Chat Messages.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>To contact</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The userId or email address of a chat contact to whom you previously sent the message. ## Delete Common Area Phone

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-common-area-phone>`

A common area phone can be provisioned by a Zoom account owner or a Zoom admin so that anyone in an
organization can use it, for example, if your office has shared desks that donât belong to a
specific employees, you could add a common area phone so that any person can use it. Use this API to
remove the
[common area phone](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066552) from
Zoom Phone System in an account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/common_area_phones/{commonAreaPhoneId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions. A
  [supported device](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060242).
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Delete Internal Call Out Country

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-internal-call-out-country>`

Delete a previously assigned
[call-out](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061683) country from a
master account or a sub account. To remove call-out country from a sub account, provide the account
ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. To remove call-out country from a master
account, provide `me` as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. This operation makes an HTTP
DELETE request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/callout_countries/{countryId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account making this API request must be a
  [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) with SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:master`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. To remove Call-out country from a sub account, provide the account ID of the sub account in the <code>accountId</code> path parameter. To remove Call-out country from a master account, provide <code>me</code> as the value of the <code>accountId</code> path parameter.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Country Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Two lettered ID of the country.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Internal Number

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-internal-number>`

This API allows a master account with SIP Connected Audio plan to delete a previously assigned
internal phone number from a master account or a sub account. To delete an internal number from a
sub account, provide the account ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. To delete
an internal number from a master account, provide `me` as the value of the `accountId` path
parameter. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/internal_numbers/{numberId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account making this API request must be a
  [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) with SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:master`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. To delete an internal number from a sub account, provide the account ID of the sub account in the <code>accountId</code> path parameter. To delete an internal number from a master account, provide <code>me</code> as the value of the <code>accountId</code> path parameter.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Number Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the phone number. This value can be retrieved by calling the List Internal Numbers API.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Location

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-location>`

Use this API to remove an emergency location. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/locations/{locationId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Delete Members Of Slg

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-members-of-slg>`

Members of the
[shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509) have
access to the groupâs phone number and voicemail. Use this API to unassign all the existing members
from a Shared Line Group. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/shared_line_groups/{sharedLineGroupId}/members endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license. A valid Shared Line Group. Account
  owner or admin privileges.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Delete Phone Numbers Slg

[shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509-Setting-up-shared-line-groups).
This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/shared_line_groups/{sharedLineGroupId}/phone_numbers endpoint.

  owner or admin privileges.

### For Configurations

- [Zoom Connector Configuration](#ZoomConfig)

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Phone Site

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-phone-site>`

Sites enable you to organize Zoom Phone users in your organization. Use this API to delete a
specific [site](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069716) in a Zoom
account. To delete a site, in the query parameter, you must provide the Site ID of another site
where the assets of current site (users, numbers and phones) can be transferred to. You cannot use
this API to delete the main site. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/sites/{siteId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account must have a Pro or a higher plan with Zoom Phone license.
  [Multiple sites](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069716-Managing-Multiple-Sites)
  must be enabled.
- Scope: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the site.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer site ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site ID of another site where the assets of the current site (users, numbers and phones) can be transferred to.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Role

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-role>`

Each Zoom user automatically has a role which can either be owner, administrator, or a member.
Account Owners and users with edit privileges for Role management can add customized roles with a
list. Use this API to delete a role. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/roles/{roleId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Pro or higher plan. For role management and updates, you must be the Account
  Owner or user with role management permissions.
- Scopes: `role:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Delete Sip Phone

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-sipphone>`

Zoomâs Phone System Integration (PSI), also referred as SIP phones, enables an organization to
leverage the Zoom client to complete a softphone registration to supported premise based PBX system.
End users will have the ability to have softphone functionality within a single client while
maintaining a comparable interface to Zoom Phone. Use this API to delete a specific SIP phone on a
Zoom account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /sip_phones/{phoneId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Currently only supported on Cisco and Avaya PBX systems. User must enable SIP Phone
  Integration by contacting the Zoom Sales team.
- Scope: `sip_phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Delete Sip Trunk

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-siptrunk>`

Use this API to remove existing SIP trunk of a sub account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE
request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/trunks/{trunkId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account making this API request must be a master account with
  [API Partner Plan](https://explore.zoom.us/docs/en-us/plan/api.html) and SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:master`

## Delete User Level Channel

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-user-level-channel>`

Zoom chat [channels](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548)
allow users to communicate via chat in private or public groups. Use this API to delete a specific
channel. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /chat/channels/{channelId} endpoint.

- Scope: `chat_channel:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note that this API only supports user-managed
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of a channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete User Setting

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:delete-user-setting>`

Use this API to remove a userâs shared voicemail access settings. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. To view these settings in your Zoom web portal, navigate to the **Admin Phone
System Management and Users Rooms interface. Click the Users tab and select \*User Settings**.
Scroll down to **Voicemail Call Recordings**. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/phone/users/{userId}/settings/{settingType} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Setting Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Corresponds to the setting item you wish to remove. ## Device Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:device-create>`

An H.323 or SIP device can make a video call to a
[Room Connector](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060661) to join
a Zoom cloud meeting. A Room Connector can also call out to an H.323 or SIP device to join a Zoom
cloud meeting. Use this API to add an H.323/SIP device to your Zoom account. This operation makes an
HTTP POST request to the /h323/devices endpoint.

- Scopes: `h323:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Device Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:device-delete>`

An H.323 or SIP device can make a video call to a
[Room Connector](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060661) to join
a Zoom cloud meeting. A Room Connector can also call out to an H.323 or SIP device to join a Zoom
cloud meeting. Use this API to delete an H.323/SIP device from your Zoom account. This operation
makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /h323/devices/{deviceId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `h323:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Device List

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:device-list>`

[Room Connector](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060661) to join
a Zoom cloud meeting. A Room Connector can also call out to an H.323 or SIP device to join a Zoom
cloud meeting. Use this API to list all H.323/SIP Devices on a Zoom account. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /h323/devices endpoint.

- Scopes: `h323:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## Download Invoice Pdf

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:download-invoice-pdf>`

Use this API to download a Zoom accountâs
[billed](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060580) invoice file, in
PDF format. To get an accountâs invoice ID, use the
[List billing invoices](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/) API. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /api/download/billing/invoices/{invoiceId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `billing:master`
- Rate Limits: You can make one request to this API every 30 minutes until the daily limit is
  reached. This API has a daily limit of 100 requests per account.

## Edit Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:edit-message>`

Use this API to edit a chat message that you previously sent to either a contact or a channel in
Zoom by providing the ID of the message as the value of the `messageId` parameter. You can get the
ID from the List Userâs Chat Messages API. Additionally, as a query parameter, you must provide
either the contactâs email address of the contact or the Channel ID of the channel where the message
was sent. For user-level apps, pass
[https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/)the-me-keyword\[the
`me` value\] instead of the `userId` parameter. Note: For an account-level
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this API can only be used on
behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983):~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit\[role\]
that has the Edit permission for Chat Messages. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/messages/{messageId} endpoint.

- Scope: `chat_message:write`,`chat_message:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## Get A Blocked List

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-ablocked-list>`

A Zoom account owner or a user with admin privilege can block phone numbers for phone users in an
account. Blocked numbers can be inbound (numbers will be blocked from calling in) and outbound
(phone users in your account wonât be able to dial those numbers). Blocked callers will hear a
generic message stating that the person they are calling is not available. Use this API to get
information about a specific blocked list. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/blocked_list/{blockedListId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account plan with Zoom phone license.
- Scope: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Get A Call Queue

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-acall-queue>`

Call queues allow you to route incoming calls to a group of users. For instance, you can use call
queues to route calls to various departments in your organization such as sales, engineering,
billing, customer service etc. Use this API to get information on a specific Call Queue. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/call_queues/{callQueueId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro, Business, or Education account. Account owner or admin permissions. Zoom Phone
  license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Get Account Billing Invoice

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-account-billing-invoice>`

Get detailed information about a specific
[invoice](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064306). To retrieve a
regular Zoom accountâs invoice details or a master accountâs invoice details, provide `me` as the
value of `accountId` path parameter. To list a sub accountâs invoice details, provide the account ID
of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /accounts/{accountId}/billing/invoices/{invoiceId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account must be enrolled in Pro or a higher plan. You can make one API request
  every thirty minutes until the daily limit is reached. This API has a daily limit of 100 requests
  per account.

## Get Account Cloud Recording

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-account-cloud-recording>`

List [cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062627)
available on an Account. To access a password protected cloud recording, add an "access_token"
parameter to the download URL and provide JWT as the value of the "access_token". This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts/{accountId}/recordings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Pro or a higher paid plan with Cloud Recording option enabled.
- Scopes: `recording:read:admin` or `account:read:admin`. If the scope `recording:read:admin` is
  used, the Account ID of the Account must be provided in the `accountId` path parameter to list
  recordings that belong to the Account. This scope only works for sub accounts. To list recordings
  of a master account, the scope must be `account:read:admin` and the value of `accountId` should be
  `me`.
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The start date in UTC for the monthly range for which you would like to retrieve recordings. The maximum range can be a month. If no value is provided for this field, the default will be the current date. For example, if you make the API request on June 30, 2020, without providing the <code>from</code> and <code>to</code> parameters, the default value of the <code>from</code> field will be 2020-06-30 and the value of the <code>to</code> field will be 2020-07-01.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The end date for the monthly range for which you would like to retrieve recordings. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Get Account Lock Settings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-account-lock-settings>`

[Account Locked Settings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065579)
allow you turn settings on or off for all users in your account. No user except the account admin or
account owner can change these settings. With lock settings, you force the settings on for all
users. Use this API to retrieve an accountâs locked settings. Note that this API can be used by Zoom
Accounts that are on a Pro or a higher plan as well accounts that have master and sub accounts
options enabled. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts/{accountId}/lock_settings
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher paid account.
- Scope: `account:read:admin`.
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. To retrieve locked settings of the master account or a regular account, provide "me" as the value of this field. To retrieve locked settings of a sub account, provide the Account ID of the sub account in this field.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>option</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p><code>meeting_security</code>: Use this query parameter to view meeting security settings applied on the account.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom query fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provide the name of the field by which you would like to filter the response. For example, if you provide "host_video" as the value of this field, you will get a response similar to the following: { "schedule_meeting": { "host_video": false } } You can provide multiple values by separating them with commas(example: "host_video,participant_video).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Get A Common Area Phone

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-acommon-area-phone>`

Use this API to get details on a specific
[common area phone](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066552) in an
account. A common area phone can be provisioned by a Zoom account owner or a Zoom admin so that
anyone in an organization can use it. For example, if your office has shared desks that donât belong
to a specific employees, you could add a common area phone so that any person can use it. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/common_area_phones/{commonAreaPhoneId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions. A
  [supported device](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060242).
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

You can get this value from the <a href="https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/">List Common Area Phones API</a>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. the /phone/external_contacts/{externalContactId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Contact Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external contactâs ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Get A Shared Line Group

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-ashared-line-group>`

A [shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509)
allows Zoom Phone admins to share a phone number and extension with a group of phone users or common
area phones. This gives members of the shared line group access to the groupâs direct phone number
and voicemail. Use this API to list all the Shared Line Groups. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /phone/shared_line_groups/{sharedLineGroupId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin privileges.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin` or `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Get A Site

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-asite>`

Sites allow you to organize Zoom Phone users in your organization. Use this API to get information
about a specific [site](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069716).
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/sites/{siteId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account must have a Pro or a higher plan with Zoom Phone license. Multiple Sites
  must be
  [enabled](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069716-Managing-Multiple-Sites#h_05c88e35-1593-491f-b1a8-b7139a75dc15).
- Scope: `phone:read:admin`

## Get Billing Invoices Reports

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-billing-invoices-reports>`

Get department billing invoices reports for a specific billing period. Provide the `billing_id` of
the billing period for which you would like to retrieve the invoices for. This ID can be retrieved
from Get Billing Reports API. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/report/billing/invoices endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Department Billing option enabled. Contact the Zoom
  Support team to enable this feature.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`, `report:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Call Log Details

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-call-log-details>`

Use this API to return information about
[call logs](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069950). This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/call_logs/{callLogId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read`, `phone:read:admin`, `phone_call_log:read`, `phone_call_log:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

## Get Call Log Metrics Details

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-call-log-metrics-details>`

Call logs provide a record of all incoming and outgoing calls over Zoom Phone in an account. Use
this API to list call log details of a specific call. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /phone/metrics/call_logs/{call_id} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, or Education account. Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`, `phone:write:admin`
- Rate Limit Label: `Light`

## Get Call Qo S

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-call-qo-s>`

Get call quality of service (QoS) data for a call made or received by a Zoom phone user in the
account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/metrics/call_logs/{callId}/qos
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, or Education account. Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`, `phone:write:admin`
