## Get Call Queue Recordings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-call-queue-recordings>`

Use this API to view
[call recordings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069433) from
the call queue. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/call_queues/{callQueueId}/recordings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license.
  [Automatic call recordings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069655)
  must be enabled in the Policy Settings for call queues.
- Scope: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Call Queue Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the call queue.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date (within a 6 month range).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date (within a 6 month range).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Channel

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-channel>`

Use this API to get information about a specific channel. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Zoom chat
[channels](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548) allow users to
communicate via chat in private or public groups. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_channel:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: For an account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this
API can only be used on behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has the View or Edit permission for Chat Channels.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of a channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the user who is the owner of the channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. ## Get Channels

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-channels>`

Use this API to list a userâs chat channels. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Zoom chat
[channels](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548) allow users to
communicate via chat in private or public groups. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/channels endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_channel:read` or `chat_channel:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note that this API supports both user-managed apps and account-level apps. However, in an
account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), to list channels of
another user in the same Zoom account, the user calling this API must have a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has the View or Edit permission for the Chat channels feature.

## Get Chat Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-chat-message>`

Get a chat message previously sent to a contact or a channel. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. You must provide one of the following query parameters: \* `to_contact` - The
email address of the Zoom contact to whom you sent the message. \* `to_channel` - The ID of the Zoom
channel where you sent the message. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/messages/{messageId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_message:read`, `chat_message:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: For an account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), you
can only use this API for a user assigned
[the Edit permission for the Chat message role setting](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>To contact</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>userId</code> or email address of a Zoom Chat contact to whom you sent the message. Note: You must use this query parameter to delete a message sent to a Zoom Chat contact.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>To channel</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>channelId</code> of the Zoom Chat channel where the message is sent. ## Get Chat Messages

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-chat-messages>`

Use this API to list the current userâs chat messages between the user and an individual contact or
a chat channel. For user-level apps, pass
[https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/)the-me-keyword\[the
`me` value\] instead of the `userId` parameter. In the query parameter, you must provide one of the
following: \* `to_contact`: The email address of the contact with whom the user conversed by sending
or receiving messages. \* `to_channel`: The channel ID of the channel to or from which the user has
sent and/or received messages. Note: For an account-level
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this API can only be used on
behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983):~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit\[role\]
that has the View or Edit permission for Chat Messages. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /chat/users/{userId}/messages endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_message:read`, `chat_message:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>To contact</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The email address of a chat contact with whom the current user chatted. Messages that were sent and/or received between the user and the contact are displayed. Note: You must provide either <code>contact</code> or <code>channel</code> as a query parameter to retrieve messages either from an individual or a chat channel.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>To channel</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The channel Id of a channel inside which the current user had chat conversations. ## Get Group Lock Settings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-group-lock-settings>`

Retrieve a [groupâs](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062584)
locked settings. If you lock a setting, the group members will not be able to modify it
individually. Note: The `force_pmi_jbh_password` field under meeting settings is planned to be
deprecated on September 22, 2019. This field will be replaced by another field that will provide the
same functionality. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /groups/{groupId}/lock_settings
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro, Business, or Education account.
- Scopes: `group:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the group.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom query fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provide the name of the field by which you would like to filter the response. For example, if you provide "host_video" as the value of this field, you will get a response similar to the following: { "schedule_meeting": { "host_video": false } } You can provide multiple values by separating them with commas(example: "host_video,participant_video).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>option</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify <code>meeting_security</code> as the value of this field if you would like to view security settings applied on a meeting hosted by the users in this group.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Get Live Stream Details For Meeting

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-live-stream-details-for-meeting>`

Zoom allows users to
[live stream a meeting](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064210)
to a custom platform. Use this API to get a meetingâs live stream configuration details such as
Stream URL, Stream Key, and Page URL. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/meetings/{meetingId}/livestream endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Meeting host must be a licensed user with a Pro or higher plan. Live streaming
  details must have been
  [configured](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064210#h_01589a6f-a40a-4e18-a448-cb746e52ebc5)
  for the meeting.
- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin` `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the meeting.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. - ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Live Stream Details For Webinar

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-live-stream-details-for-webinar>`

Zoom allows users to
[live stream a webinar](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064210)
to a custom platform. Use this API to get a webinarâs live stream configuration details such as
Stream URL, Stream Key, and Page URL. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/webinars/{webinarId}/livestream endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on. Live streaming details must have been
  [configured](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064210#h_01589a6f-a40a-4e18-a448-cb746e52ebc5)
  for the webinar.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin` `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinarâs unique ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output.   privileges.

## Get Plan Usage

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-plan-usage>`

Get information on usage of [plans](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/references/billing-plans/)
of an account. This API supports regular accounts as well as master and sub accounts. To get the
plan usage of a regular account, use the `account:read:admin` scope and provide `me` as the value of
the `accountId` path parameter. To get plan usage of a master account, provide the keyword "me" as
the value of the `accountId` path parameter and use the `billing:master` scope. To get plan usage of
a sub account, provide the actual account Id of the sub account as the value of the `accountId` path
parameter and use the `billing:master` scope. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/accounts/{accountId}/plans/usage endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account type: master account on a paid Pro, Business, or Enterprise plan.
- Scope: `billing:master` for master and sub accounts. `account:read:admin` for regular Zoom
  accounts.
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Account ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Ps Operation Logs

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-psoperation-logs>`

The Phone System operation logs report allows account owners and admins to view monthly Zoom phone
related admin operation details. Use this API to retrieve the Phone System Operation Logs Report.
Account owners and admins can also access this information by logging into their Zoom accounts and
navigating to
[phone system operation logs](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069964).
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/reports/operationlogs endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account must be enrolled in Pro or a higher plan. Account must be enrolled in a
  [Zoom phone](https://zoom.us/pricing/zoom-phone) plan.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`, `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date in 'yyyy-mm-dd' format. By default, the value of this field is "all" and thus, the response will include log of all operations for the defined period. ## Get Role Information

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-role-information>`

Each Zoom user automatically has a role which can either be owner, administrator, or a member.
Account Owners and users with edit privileges for Role management can add customized roles with a
list of privileges. Use this API to get information including specific privileges assigned to a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983). This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /roles/{roleId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Pro or higher plan. For role management and updates, you must be the Account
  Owner or user with role management permissions.
- Scopes: `role:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Get Tracking Sources

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-tracking-sources>`

[Webinar Registration Tracking Sources](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065543)
allow you to see where your registrants are coming from if you share the webinar registration page
in multiple platforms. You can then use the source tracking to see the number of registrants
generated from each platform. Use this API to list information on all the tracking sources of a
webinar. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/tracking_sources
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Webinar license. Registration must be required for the Webinar.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin`, `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID in "<strong>long</strong>" format (represented as int64 data type in JSON).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get User Contact

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-user-contact>`

A user under an organizationâs Zoom account has internal users listed under Company Contacts in the
Zoom Client. A Zoom user can also add another Zoom user as a
[contact](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065609). Call this API
to get information on a specific contact of the Zoom user. Note that this API only supports
user-managed [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/). This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /chat/users/me/contacts/{contactId} endpoint.

- Scope: `chat_contact:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Contact Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The userâs contact ID or email address. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Get User Contacts

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-user-contacts>`

A user under an organizationâs Zoom account has internal users listed under Company Contacts in the
Zoom Client. A Zoom user can also add another Zoom user as a
[contact](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065609). Call this API
to list all the contacts of a Zoom user. Zoom contacts are categorized into "company contacts" and
"external contacts". You must specify the contact type in the `type` query parameter. If you do not
specify, by default, the type will be set as company contact. Note that this API only supports
user-managed [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/). This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /chat/users/me/contacts endpoint.

- Scope: `chat_contact:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. ## Get User Level Channel

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-user-level-channel>`

Zoom chat [channels](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548)
allow users to communicate via chat in private or public groups. Use this API to get information
about a specific channel. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /chat/channels/{channelId}
endpoint.

- Scope: `chat_channel:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note that this API supports only user-managed
[OAuth apps](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/). If you use an Account-Level
OAuth Access token, you can only retrieve the channel information for the authorized user. You canât
retrieve the channel information of other account users. Use the
[Account-Level Get Channel Info API](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/) to retrieve the channel
information of other account users.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of a channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Get Zr Account Profile

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-zraccount-profile>`

Get details on the account profile of a Zoom Room. This information can only by accessed either by
the Zoom Room Account Owner or a user with Zoom Rooms admin permission. To get information on an
individual Room Profile, use [Get Zoom Room Profile API](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/)
instead. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rooms/account_profile endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Zoom account owner or Zoom Rooms admin permissions.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Zr Account Settings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-zraccount-settings>`

Get details on Account Settings of a Zoom Room. With this API, you can view either the Account
Meeting Settings or the Alert Settings (Client Alert Settings and Notfication Settings) of the Zoom
Rooms account. By default, only Account Meeting Settings are returned. To view only Alert Settings,
specify `alert` as the value of the `setting_type` query parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /rooms/account_settings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Zoom Room licenses. Owner or Admin privileges on the Zoom Account.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Setting type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MEETING</p></li><li><p>ALERT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of setting that you would like to retrieve:</p><p>* <code>meeting</code></p><p>Meeting settings of the Zoom Rooms Account.</p><p>* <code>alert</code></p><p>Alert Settings applied on the Zoom Rooms Account.</p><p>* <code>signage</code></p><p>View digital signage settings of the Zoom Rooms Account.</p></td><td><p>MEETING</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Zr Location Profile

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-zrlocation-profile>`

Each location type of the
[Zoom Rooms Location Hierarchy](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063345)
has a profile page that includes information such as name of the location, address, or support
email. Use this API to retrieve information about a specific Zoom Rooms location type such as
information about the city where the Zoom Rooms is located. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /rooms/locations/{locationId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account owner or admin permission. Zoom Rooms version 4.0 or higher.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

the value of the `setting_type` query parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the

- Scopes: `room:read:admin`

applied on the Zoom Rooms in an account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the

- Prerequisites: Zoom Rooms version 4.0 or higher. Account owner or admin permissions.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Get Zr Profile

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-zrprofile>`

Zoom Rooms is a software-based room system that provides an integrated experience for audio
conferencing, wireless screen sharing and video conferencing. Use this API to get detailed
information on a specific Zoom Room in a Zoom account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /rooms/{roomId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with Zoom Room license.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## Get Zr Settings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:get-zrsettings>`

Get information on meeting or alert settings applied to a specific Zoom Room. By default, only
**Meeting Settings** are returned. To view only Alert Settings, specify `alert` as the value of the
`setting_type` query parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/rooms/{roomId}/settings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Zoom Room licenses. Owner or Admin privileges on the Zoom Account.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## Group Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:group-create>`

Create a [group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062584) under an
account. You can add a maximum of 100 groups in one account per day. If you go over, you will get an
error. You can add a maximum of 5000 groups in one account. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /groups endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account.
- Scopes: `group:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Group Locked Settings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:group-locked-settings>`

Update a [groupâs](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062584) locked
settings. If you lock a setting, the group members will not be able to modify it individually. Note
that the `force_pmi_jbh_password` field under meeting settings is planned to be deprecated on
September 22, 2019. This field will be replaced by another field that will provide the same
functionality. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /groups/{groupId}/lock_settings
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro, Business, or Education account.
- Scopes: `group:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## Group Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:group-update>`

Update a [group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062584) under
your account. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /groups/{groupId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro, Business, or Education account.
- Scopes: `group:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Im Chat Messages

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:im-chat-messages>`

Retrieve IM chat messages for a specified period of time. This API only supports oauth2. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /im/chat/sessions/{sessionId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `imchat:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Deprecated: By the end of 2021, Zoom will deprecate this API in favor of a consolidated set of APIs.
The API will still be available for you to use, though Zoom will no longer provide support for it.
For further information, refer to
[Announcements: IM APIs Deprecation](https://developers.zoom.us/changelog/).

## Im Group

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:im-group>`

makes an HTTP GET request to the /im/groups/{groupId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `imgroup:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The group ID. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## In Meeting Recording Control

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:in-meeting-recording-control>`

Use this API to control the
[in-meeting](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060186) recording
features, such as starting a recording, stopping a recording, pausing a recording, and resuming a
recording. This API only works for Cloud Recordings and not for local recordings. This operation
makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /live_meetings/{meetingId}/events endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The meeting must be a live meeting. Cloud Recording must be enabled. The user using
  this API must either be the host or alternative host of the meeting.
- Scopes: `meeting:write`, `meeting:write:admin`, `meeting:master`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the live meeting.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Invite Channel Members

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:invite-channel-members>`

Use this API to invite members that are in a userâs contact list to a channel. For user-level apps,
pass [the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead
of the `userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}/members endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_channel:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: For an account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this
API can only be used on behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has the Edit permission for Chat Channels.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Channel ID: Unique identifier of the channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the user who is the owner of this channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Join Channel

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:join-channel>`

A [channel](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548) can have one
or multiple members. Use this API to join a channel that is open for anyone in the same organization
to join. You cannot use this API to join private channels that only allows invited members to be a
part of it. Note that this API only supports user-managed
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/). This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /chat/channels/{channelId}/members/me endpoint.

- Scope: `chat_channel:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## Leave Channel

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:leave-channel>`

If youâre no longer interested in being a member of an existing channel, you can leave the channel
at any time. Use this API to leave a specific channel. After leaving the channel, you can no longer
access information from that channel. Note that this API only supports user-managed
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/). This operation makes an HTTP
DELETE request to the /chat/channels/{channelId}/members/me endpoint.

- Scope: `chat_channel:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## List Account Phone Numbers

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-account-phone-numbers>`

Use this API to list all Zoom Phone numbers in a Zoom account. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /phone/numbers endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Pro or higher account plan. A Zoom phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ASSIGNED</p></li><li><p>UNASSIGNED</p></li><li><p>ALL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Query response by number assignment. The value can be one of the following:</p><p>* <code>assigned</code></p><p>The number has been assigned to either a user, a call queue, an auto-receptionist, or a common area phone in an account.</p><p>* <code>unassigned</code></p><p>The number is not assigned to anyone.</p><p>* <code>all</code></p><p>Include both assigned and unassigned numbers in the response.</p><p>* <code>byoc</code></p><p>Include Bring Your Own Carrier (BYOC) numbers only in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Extension type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USER</p></li><li><p>CALL_QUEUE</p></li><li><p>AUTO_RECEPTIONIST</p></li><li><p>COMMON_AREA_PHONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of assignee to whom the number is assigned. The value can be one of the following: <code>user</code> <code>callQueue</code> <code>autoReceptionist</code> <code>commonAreaPhone</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Number type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TOLL</p></li><li><p>TOLLFREE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of phone number. The value can be either <code>toll</code> or <code>tollfree</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pending numbers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Include or exclude pending numbers in the response. The value can be either <code>true</code> or <code>false</code>.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the Site. Use this query parameter if you have enabled multiple sites and would like to filter the response of this API call by a specific phone site. See [Managing multiple sites](<a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069716">https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069716</a>) or [Adding a site](<a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069716#h_05c88e35-1593-491f-b1a8-b7139a75dc15">https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069716#h_05c88e35-1593-491f-b1a8-b7139a75dc15</a>) for details.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Archived Files

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-archived-files>`

Zoomâs
[archiving solution](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067188)
allows account administrators to set up an automated mechanism to record, collect and archive
meeting data to a 3rd party platform of their choice and hence, satisfy FINRA and/ or other
compliance requirements. Use this API to retrieve the archived meeting or webinar files of an
account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /archive_files endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Enable cloud recording. Follow the
  [enablement process](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067188#h_01ENPBD3WR68D7FAKTBY92SG45)
  to access the archiving feature.
- Scope: `recording:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date for the query in "yyyy-MM-dd''T''HH:mm:ss''Z''" format. The duration for the query defined using the "from" and "to" parameters should not exceed 7 days as this API only provides a weekâs data at once.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date for the query in "yyyy-MM-dd''T''HH:mm:ss''Z''" format.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query date type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MEETING_START_TIME</p></li><li><p>ARCHIVE_COMPLETE_TIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The query date type for the <code>from</code> and <code>to</code> parameters.</p></td><td><p>MEETING_START_TIME</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## List Blocked List

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-blocked-list>`

A Zoom account owner or a user with admin privilege can block phone numbers for phone users in an
account. Blocked numbers can be inbound (numbers will be blocked from calling in) and outbound
(phone users in your account wonât be able to dial those numbers). Blocked callers will hear a
generic message stating that the person they are calling is not available. Use this API to list all
the blocked lists in an acccount. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/blocked_list endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account plan with Zoom phone license.
- Scope: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## List Byocsip Trunk

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-byocsiptrunk>`

Use this API to return a list of an accountâs assigned
[BYOC (Bring Your Own Carrier) SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunks](https://explore.zoom.us/docs/doc/Zoom-Bring%20Your%20Own%20Carrier.pdf).
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/sip_trunk/trunks endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin` or `phone:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## List Call Logs Metrics

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-call-logs-metrics>`

Call logs provide a record of all incoming and outgoing calls over Zoom Phone in an account. Use
this API to list monthly call logs metrics. You can use query parameters to filter the response by
date, site and MOS(Mean Opinion Score) of the call. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/metrics/call_logs endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, or Education account. Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`, `phone:write:admin`
- Rate Limit Label: `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date for the report in <code>yyyy-mm-dd</code> format. Specify a 30 day range using the <code>from</code> and <code>to</code> parameters as the response provides a maximum of a month worth of data per API request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date for the report in <code>yyyy-mm-dd</code> format.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the <a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069716">site</a>. Use this query parameter if you have enabled multiple sites and would like to filter the response of this API call by call logs of a specific phone site.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quality type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter call logs by voice quality. Zoom uses MOS of 3.5 as a general baseline to categorize calls by call quality. A MOS greater than or equal to 3.5 means good quality, while below 3.5 means poor quality. The value of this field can be one of the following:</p><p>* <code>good</code></p><p>Retrieve call logs of the call(s) with good quality of voice.</p><p>* <code>bad</code></p><p>Retrieve call logs of the call(s) with good quality of voice.</p><p>* <code>all</code></p><p>Retrieve all call logs without filtering by voice quality.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of records returned within a single call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Call Queues

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-call-queues>`

Call queues allow you to route incoming calls to a group of users. For instance, you can use call
queues to route calls to various departments in your organization such as sales, engineering,
billing, customer service, etc. Use this API to list Call queues. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /phone/call_queues endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro, Business, or Education account. Account owner or admin permissions. Zoom Phone
  license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## List Calling Plans

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-calling-plans>`

Use this API to return all of an accountâs Zoom Phone
[calling plans](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/references/billing-plans/#zoom-phone-calling-plans).

- Prerequisites: A Pro or a higher account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
