## List Channel Members

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-channel-members>`

Use this API to list all members of a channel. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}/members endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_channel:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: For an account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this
API can only be used on behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has the View or Edit permission for Chat Channels.

## List Common Area Phones

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-common-area-phones>`

Use this API to list all of an accountâs
[common area phones](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066552). A
common area phone can be provisioned by a Zoom account owner or a Zoom admin so that anyone in an
organization can use it. For example, if your office has shared desks that donât belong to a
specific employees, you could add a common area phone so that any person can use it. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/common_area_phones endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions. A
  [supported device](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060242).
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The total number of records returned from a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Digital Signage Content

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-digital-signage-content>`

List information about existing
[Zoom Rooms digital signage](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060299)
content in a Zoom account. You can also access this information by logging into your Zoom account in
the Zoom web portal and visiting the Digital Signage Content page listed under Room Management. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rooms/digital_signage endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Rooms. Existing content files or folder in the
  Digital Signage Content page.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify the type of digital signage resource. ## List External Contacts

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-external-contacts>`

Use this API to list external contacts. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/external_contacts endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## List Internal Callout Countries

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-internal-callout-countries>`

Retrieve the list of internal
[call-out](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061683) countries of a
master account or a sub account. To list call-out enabled countries of a sub account, provide the
account ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. To list call-out enabled countries
of a master account, provide `me` as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/callout_countries endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account making this API request must be a
  [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) with SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:master`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. ## List Internal Numbers

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-internal-numbers>`

This API allows a master account with SIP Connected Audio plan to list internal phone numbers, such
as for numbers that are not provided by Zoom but are owned by the organization consuming the API,
assigned to a master account or a sub account. To list the internal numbers of a sub account,
provide the account ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. To list internal
numbers of a master account, provide `me` as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/internal_numbers
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account making this API request must be a
  [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) with SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:master`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. To list the internal numbers of a sub account, provide the account ID of the sub account in the <code>accountId</code> path parameter. To list the internal numbers of a master account, provide <code>me</code> as the value of the <code>accountId</code> path parameter.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Locations

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-locations>`

Use this API to list emergency service locations. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/locations endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## List Meeting Satisfaction

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-meeting-satisfaction>`

If the
[End of Meeting Feedback Survey](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067520)
option is enabled, attendees will be prompted with a survey window where they can tap either the
**Thumbs Up** or **Thumbs Down** button that indicates their Zoom meeting experience. With this API,
you can get information on the attendees' meeting satisfaction. Specify a monthly date range for the
query using the from and to query parameters. The month should fall within the last six months. To
get information on the survey results with negative experiences (indicated by **Thumbs Down**), use
the [Get Zoom Meetings Client Feedback API](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/). This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /metrics/client/satisfaction endpoint.

- Scopes: `dashboard:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The start date for the query in the yyyy-mm-dd format.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The end date for the query in the yyyy-mm-dd format.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## List Meeting Templates

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-meeting-templates>`

Use this API to list
[meeting templates](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067229) that
are available to use by a user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/users/{userId}/meeting_templates endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:read` or `meeting:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## List Past Meeting Files

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-past-meeting-files>`

Note that this API has been deprecated and is no longer supported due to GCM encryption updates for
security purposes. To learn about saving the in-meeting chat files via Zoom Client, refer to the
[Saving in-meeting chat](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067312)
guide. List files sent via in-meeting chat during a meeting. The in-meeting files are deleted after
24 hours of the meeting completion time. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/past_meetings/{meetingId}/files endpoint.

- Scope: `meeting:read`, `meeting:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID in <code>long</code> format. The data type of this field is <code>long</code>, which is represented as int64 in JSON. While storing the meeting ID in your database, store it as a <code>long</code> data type and not as an integer, as the meeting IDs can be longer than 10 digits.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Past Meeting Polls

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-past-meeting-polls>`

[Polls](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066150) allow the meeting
host to survey attendees. Use this API to list poll results of a meeting. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /past_meetings/{meetingId}/polls endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Host user type must be Pro. Meeting must be a scheduled meeting. Instant meetings
  do not have polling features enabled.
- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin`, `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID or the meeting UUID. If a meeting ID is provided in the request instead of a UUID, the response will be for the latest meeting instance. If a UUID starts with "/" or contains "//" (example: "/ajXp112QmuoKj4854875==\"), you must double encode the UUID before making an API request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Past Webinar Files

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-past-webinar-files>`

Note that this API has been deprecated and is no longer supported due to GCM encryption updates for
security purposes. List files sent via in-meeting chat during a meeting. The in-meeting files are
deleted after 24 hours of the meeting completion time. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /past_webinars/{webinarId}/files endpoint.

- Scope: `webinar:read`, `webinar:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## List Past Webinar Poll Results

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-past-webinar-poll-results>`

The polling feature for a webinar enables you to create single choice or multiple choice polling
questions for your webinars. Use this API to retrieve the results for Webinar Polls of a specific
Webinar. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /past_webinars/{webinarId}/polls endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Webinar license
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin`, `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## List Past Webinar Qa

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-past-webinar-qa>`

The
[Question & Answer (Q&A)](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064380)
feature for Webinars allows attendees to ask questions during the Webinar and for the panelists,
co-hosts and host to answer their questions. Use this API to list Q&A of a specific Webinar. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /past_webinars/{webinarId}/qa endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Webinar license
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin`, `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Phone Devices

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-phone-devices>`

List all the
[desk phone devices](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061431) that
are configured with Zoom Phone on an account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/devices endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin
  permissions.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ASSIGNED</p></li><li><p>UNASSIGNED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>State of the device. [sites](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069716) that have been

  [enabled](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0069716-Managing-Multiple-Sites#h_05c88e35-1593-491f-b1a8-b7139a75dc15).

## List Setting Templates

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-setting-templates>`

Use this API to get a list of all the created phone template settings. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /phone/setting_templates endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin` or `phone:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returns within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the Site. This field is required only if multiple sites have been enabled. of the site. Required only when multiple sites are enabled. See [Managing multiple sites](<a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069716">https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0069716</a>) for details. If this is not provided, the response lists the account level setting templates.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## List Shared Line Groups

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-shared-line-groups>`

A [shared line group](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066509)
allows Zoom Phone admins to share a phone number and extension with a group of phone users or common
area phones. This gives members of the shared line group access to the groupâs direct phone number
and voicemail. Use this API to list all the Shared Line Groups. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /phone/shared_line_groups endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher account with Zoom Phone license. Account owner or admin privileges.
- Scopes: `phone:read:admin`, `phone:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## List Sip Phones

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-sip-phones>`

Zoomâs Phone System Integration (PSI), also referred as SIP phones, enables an organization to
leverage the Zoom client to complete a softphone registration to supported premise based PBX system.
End users will have the ability to have softphone functionality within a single client while
maintaining a comparable interface to Zoom Phone. Use this API to list SIP phones on an account.
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /sip_phones endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Currently only supported on Cisco and Avaya PBX systems. User must enable SIP Phone
  Integration by contacting the Zoom Sales team.
- Scope: `sip_phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## List Sip Trunk Numbers

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-sip-trunk-numbers>`

With SIP-connected audio, Zoom establishes a SIP trunk (a network connection specifically designed
to make and deliver phone calls) over a direct and private connection between the customerâs network
and the Zoom cloud. Meeting participants that dial into a meeting or have the meeting call them, and
are On-Net from the perspective of the customers' IP telephony network, will be connected over this
trunk rather than over the PSTN. Use this API to list all the numbers that are configured for SIP
Connected Audio in a Zoom Account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/sip_trunk/numbers endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with SIP Connected Audio plan enabled. The account must be
  a master account.
- Scopes: `sip_trunk:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Sip Trunks

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-siptrunks>`

With SIP-connected audio, Zoom establishes a SIP trunk (a network connection specifically designed
to make and deliver phone calls) over a direct and private connection between the customerâs network
and the Zoom cloud. Meeting participants that dial into a meeting or have the meeting call them, and
are On-Net from the perspective of the customerâs IP telephony network, will be connected over this
trunk rather than over the PSTN. Use this API to list all the SIP trunks assigned to a master
account or a sub account of the master account. To retrieve SIP trunks assigned to a sub account,
provide the account ID of the sub account in the `accountId` path parameter. To retrieve SIP trunks
of a master account, provide `me` as the value of the `accountId` path parameter. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /accounts/{accountId}/sip_trunk/trunks endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account must either be a master account or a sub account with
  [API Partner Plan](https://explore.zoom.us/docs/en-us/plan/api.html) and SIP Connected Audio Plan.
- Scope: `sip_trunk:read:admin`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the account. To retrieve SIP trunks assigned to a sub account, provide the account ID of the sub account in the as the value of this field. To retrieve SIP trunks of a master account, provide <code>me</code> as the value of this field.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List Webinar Templates

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-webinar-templates>`

Use this API to list a userâs existing
[webinar templates'](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064225)
information. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. When you schedule a webinar, you can save the settings for that webinar as a
template for scheduling future webinars. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/users/{userId}/webinar_templates endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher account with Webinar plan enabled.

## List Zoom Rooms

conferencing, wireless screen sharing and video conferencing. Use this API to list all the existing
[Zoom Rooms](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063391) in a Zoom
account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rooms endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with Zoom Room license.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## List Zr Devices

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-zrdevices>`

List information about the devices that are being used for a specific
[Zoom Room](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063391) in an
account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rooms/{roomId}/devices endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with Zoom Room license. Account owner or admin permissions.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## List Zr Locations

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:list-zrlocations>`

A Zoom account owner or a Zoom Room administrator can establish a
[location hierarchy](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063345) to
help manage Zoom Rooms that are spread among a variety of locations. Use this API to list the
different location types used for Zoom Rooms in an account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /rooms/locations endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Account owner or admin permissions. Zoom Rooms Version 4.0 or higher.
- Scopes: `room:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## Listimmessages

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:listimmessages>`

Get IM Chat messages for a specified period of time. This API only supports Oauth2. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /im/users/{userId}/chat/messages endpoint.

- Scopes: `imchat:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Deprecated: By the end of 2021, Zoom will deprecate this API in favor of a consolidated set of APIs.
The API will still be available for you to use, though Zoom will no longer provide support for it.
For further information, refer to
[Announcements: IM APIs Deprecation](https://developers.zoom.us/changelog/).

## Manage E911signage

[Zoom Rooms digital signage](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060299).

  higher. Zoom Rooms digital signage must be
  [enabled](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060299).

## Mark Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:mark-message>`

Mark a message as read or unread. For user-level apps, pass
[https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/)the-me-keyword\[the
`me` value\] instead of the `userId` parameter. Note that for an account-level
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this API can only be used on
behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983):~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit\[role\]
that has the Edit permission for Chat Messages. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/messages/{messageId}/status endpoint.

- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the message.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Meeting

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting>`

Retrieve the details of a meeting. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/meetings/{meetingId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin` `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID in <code>long</code> format. ## Meeting Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-create>`

[Create a meeting](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060700) for a
user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This API has a daily rate limit of 100 requests per day. Therefore, only 100
**Create a Meeting** API requests are permitted within a 24 hour window for a user. The start_url of
a meeting is a URL using which a host or an alternative host can start a meeting. The expiration
time for the start_url field is two hours for all regular users. For custCreate meeting hosts (
i.e., users created using the custCreate option via the
[Create Users API](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/), the expiration time of the start_url field
is 90 days from the generation of the start_url. For security reasons, the recommended way to
retrieve the updated value for the start_url field programmatically (after expiry) is by calling the
[Retrieve a Meeting API](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/meetings/) and referring to the value
of the start_url field in the response. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/users/{userId}/meetings endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:write:admin` `meeting:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Meeting Invitation

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-invitation>`

Retrieve the meeting invite note that was sent for a specific meeting. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/invitation endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin` `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID in <code>long</code> format. The data type of this field is <code>long</code>, which is represented as int64 in JSON. While storing the meeting ID in your database, store it as a <code>long</code> data type and not as an integer, as the meeting IDs can be longer than 10 digits.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Meeting Poll Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-poll-create>`

Polls allow the meeting host to survey attendees. Use this API to create a
[poll](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066150) for a meeting.
This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/polls endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Host user type must be a Pro or higher plan. Polling feature must be enabled in the
  hostâs account. Meeting must be a scheduled meeting. Instant meetings do not have polling features
  enabled.
- Scopes: `meeting:write:admin` `meeting:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID in <code>long</code> format. The data type of this field is <code>long</code>, which is represented as int64 in JSON. While storing the meeting ID in your database, store it as a <code>long</code> data type and not as an integer, as the meeting IDs can be longer than 10 digits.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Meeting Poll Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-poll-delete>`

Polls allow the meeting host to survey attendees. Use this API to delete a meeting
[poll](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066150). This operation
makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/polls/{pollId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Host user type must be Pro. Polling feature should be enabled in the hostâs
  account. Meeting must be a scheduled meeting. Instant meetings do not have polling features
  enabled.
- Scopes: `meeting:write:admin` `meeting:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Meeting Polls

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-polls>`

Polls allow the meeting host to survey attendees. Use this API to list the
[polls](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066150) of a meeting.
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/polls endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Host user type must be a Pro or higher plan. Meeting must be a scheduled meeting.
  Instant meetings do not have polling features enabled.

## Meeting Recording Registrant Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-recording-registrant-create>`

Cloud Recordings of past Zoom Meetings can be made
[on-demand](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067514). Users should
be
[registered](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/meetings/#tag/cloud-recording/POST/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/registrants)
to view these recordings. Use this API to register a user to gain access to the **On-demand Cloud
Recordings** of a past meeting. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/registrants endpoint.

- Scopes: `recording:write:admin`, `recording:write`.
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

be
[registered](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/meetings/#tag/cloud-recording/POST/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/registrants)
to view these recordings. Use this API to list the registrants of **On-demand Cloud Recordings** of
a past meeting. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/registrants endpoint.

- Scopes: `recording:read:admin`, `recording:read`.
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Meeting Registrant Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-registrant-create>`

Register a participant for a meeting. Note that there is a maximum limit of 4999 registrants per
meeting and users will see an error if the capacity is reached. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/registrants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Host user type must be "Licensed".
- Scopes: `meeting:write:admin` `meeting:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID in <code>long</code> format. ## Meeting Registrants

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-registrants>`

A host or a user with admin permission can require
[registration for a Zoom meeting](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).
Use this API to list users that have registered for a meeting. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/registrants endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin` `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID in <code>long</code> format. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Meeting Registrants Questions Get

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-registrants-questions-get>`

List registration questions that will be displayed to users while
[registering for a meeting](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065026).
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/registrants/questions
endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:read`, `meeting:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Meeting Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meeting-update>`

Update the details of a meeting. This API has a rate limit of 100 requests per day. Because of this,
a meeting can only be updated for a maximum of 100 times within a 24 hour period. This operation
makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /meetings/{meetingId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:write:admin`, `meeting:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID in <code>long</code> format. - ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Meetings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:meetings>`

List all the meetings that were scheduled for a user (meeting host). For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This API only supports scheduled meetings. This API does not return information
about instant meetings. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{userId}/meetings
endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin`, `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>SCHEDULED</p></li><li><p>LIVE</p></li><li><p>UPCOMING</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting types: <code>scheduled</code> - This includes all valid past meetings (unexpired), live meetings and upcoming scheduled meetings. It is equivalent to the combined list of "Previous Meetings" and "Upcoming Meetings" displayed in the userâs [Meetings page](<a href="https://www.zoom.com/en/products/virtual-meetings/">https://www.zoom.com/en/products/virtual-meetings/</a>) on the Zoom Web Portal. <code>live</code> - All the ongoing meetings. <code>upcoming</code> - All upcoming meetings including live meetings.</p></td><td><p>LIVE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. ## Participant Feedback

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:participant-feedback>`

When a meeting ends, each attendee will be prompted to share their meeting experience by clicking
either thumbs up or thumbs down. Use this API to retrieve the feedback submitted for a specific
meeting. Note that this API only works for meetings scheduled after December 20, 2020. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /metrics/meetings/{meetingId}/participants/satisfaction
endpoint.

- Prerequisites:
  [Feedback to Zoom](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0068808)
  setting must be enabled by the participant prior to the meeting. The user making the API request
  must be enrolled in a Business or a higher plan.
- Scope: `dashboard_meetings:read:admiin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting ID or the meeting UUID. If a meeting ID is provided in the request instead of a UUID, the response will be for the latest meeting instance. If a UUID starts with "/" or contains "//" (example: "/ajXp112QmuoKj4854875==\"), you must double encode the UUID before making an API request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PAST</p></li><li><p>PAST_ONE</p></li><li><p>LIVE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Specify a value to get the response for the corresponding meeting type. The value of this field can be one of the following: <code>past</code> - Meeting that already occurred in the specified date range. <code>pastOne</code> - Past meetings that were attended by only one user. <code>live</code> - Live meetings. If you do not provide this field, the default value will be <code>live</code> and thus, the API will only query responses for live meetings.</p></td><td><p>LIVE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams.   setting must be enabled by the participant prior to the meeting. The user making the API request
  must be enrolled in a Business or a higher plan.

## Past Meeting Details

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:past-meeting-details>`

Get details on a past meeting. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/past_meetings/{meetingUUID} endpoint.

- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin` `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

Note: Please double encode your UUID when using this API if the UUID begins with a '/'or contains
'//' in it.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting UUID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meetingâs universally unique identifier (UUID). If the meeting UUID begins with a <code>/</code> character or contains a <code>//</code> character, you <strong>must</strong> double-encode the meeting UUID when using the meeting UUID for other API calls.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Past Meeting Participants

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:past-meeting-participants>`

Retrieve information on participants from a past meeting. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /past_meetings/{meetingUUID}/participants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Paid account on a Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `meeting:read:admin` `meeting:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: Please double encode your UUID when using this API if the UUID begins with a '/'or contains
'//' in it.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting UUID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meetingâs universally unique identifier (UUID).
