## Phone User

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:phone-user>`

Use this API to return a userâs
[Zoom phone](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060257) profile. For
user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/users/{userId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read`, `phone:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Phone User Call Logs

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:phone-user-call-logs>`

Use this API to get a userâs
[Zoom phone](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060257) call logs.
For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /phone/users/{userId}/call_logs
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read`, `phone:read:admin`, `phone_call_log:read`, `phone_call_log:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. The date range defined by the "from" and "to" parameters should only be one month as the report includes only one month worth of data at once.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>MISSED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>type</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Phone number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter API responses to include call logs of only the phone number defined in this field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>START_TIME</p></li><li><p>END_TIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enables you to sort call logs by start or end time. ## Phone User Voice Mails

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:phone-user-voice-mails>`

Use this API to get a userâs Zoom Phone voicemails. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/phone/users/{userId}/voice_mails endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account. A Zoom Phone license.
- Scopes: `phone:read`, `phone:read:admin`, `phone_voicemail:read`, `phone_voicemail:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

## Post Phone Sip Trunk

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:post-phone-siptrunk>`

A [master account](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/ma/) owner can use this API to assign SIP
(Session Initiation Protocol) trunks to a subaccount. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to
the /accounts/{accountId}/phone/sip_trunk/trunks endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Business or Enterprise account.
- Scopes: `phone:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## React Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:react-message>`

Use this API to react (add or remove) to a chat message with an emoji. For an account-level
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this API can only be used on
behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has the Edit permission for Chat Messages. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/messages/{messageId}/emoji_reactions endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_message:write`, `chat_message:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

- Prerequisites: Cloud Recording should be enabled on the userâs account.

## Recording Delete One

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:recording-delete-one>`

Delete a specific recording file from a meeting. Note that to use this API, you must enable the "The
host can delete cloud recordings" setting. You can find this setting in the Recording tab of the
Settings interface in the [Zoom web portal](https://www.zoom.com/). This operation makes an HTTP
DELETE request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/{recordingId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `recording:write:admin`, `recording:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>To get Cloud Recordings of a meeting, provide the meeting ID or meeting UUID. If the meeting ID is provided instead of UUID, the response will be for the latest meeting instance. To get Cloud Recordings of a webinar, provide the webinar ID or the webinar UUID. If the webinar ID is provided instead of UUID,the response will be for the latest webinar instance. If a UUID starts with "/" or contains "//" (example: "/ajXp112QmuoKj4854875=="), you must double encode the UUID before making an API request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recording Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The recording ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TRASH</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The recording delete actions:</p><p>* <code>trash</code></p><p>Move recording to trash.</p><p>* <code>delete</code></p><p>Delete recording permanently.</p></td><td><p>TRASH</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Recording Get

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:recording-get>`

Get all the
[recordings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062627#h_7420acb5-1897-4061-87b4-5b76e99c03b4)
from a meeting or Webinar instance. The recording files can be downloaded via the `download_url`
property listed in the response. To access a password-protected cloud recording, add an
`access_token` parameter to the download URL and provide OAuth access token or JWT as the
`access_token` value. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings endpoint.

- Scopes: `recording:read:admin`, `recording:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>To get Cloud Recordings of a meeting, provide the meeting ID or meeting UUID. If the meeting ID is provided instead of UUID, the response will be for the latest meeting instance. To get Cloud Recordings of a webinar, provide the webinar ID or the webinar UUID. If the webinar ID is provided instead of UUID,the response will be for the latest webinar instance. If a UUID starts with "/" or contains "//" (example: "/ajXp112QmuoKj4854875=="), you must double encode the UUID before making an API request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Get the <code>download_access_token</code> field for downloading meeting recordings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>ttl</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time to live (TTL) of the <code>download_access_token</code>. This is only valid if the <code>include_fields</code> query parameter contains <code>download_access_token</code>. The range is between 0-604800.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Recording Registrant Question Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:recording-registrant-question-update>`

For [on-demand](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067514) meeting
recordings, you can include fields with questions that will be shown to registrants when they
register to view the recording. Use this API to update registration questions that are to be
answered by users while registering to view a recording. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request
to the /meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/registrants/questions endpoint.

- Scopes: `recording:write:admin`, `recording:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Recording Setting Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:recording-setting-update>`

Retrieve settings applied to a meetingâs
[Cloud Recording](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062627). This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/settings endpoint.

- Scopes: `recording:read:admin` `recording:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Meeting Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>To get Cloud Recordings of a meeting, provide the meeting ID or meeting UUID. If the webinar ID is provided instead of UUID,the response will be for the latest webinar instance. If a UUID starts with "/" or contains "//" (example: "/ajXp112QmuoKj4854875=="), you must double encode the UUID before making an API request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Recording Status Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:recording-status-update>`

Zoom allows users to recover recordings from trash for up to 30 days from the deletion date. Use
this API to recover all deleted
[cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062627) of a
specific meeting. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/status endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Pro user with Cloud Recording enabled.
- Scopes: `recording:write:admin` `recording:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Recordings List

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:recordings-list>`

Use this API to list all
[cloud recordings](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0062627) of a
user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Note that to access a userâs password protected cloud recording, add an
access_token parameter to the download URL and provide either the JWT or the userâs OAuth access
token as the value of the access_token parameter. When a user records a meeting or a webinar by
choosing the **Record to the Cloud** option, the video, audio, and chat text are recorded in the
Zoom cloud. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{userId}/recordings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan. Cloud Recording must be enabled on the userâs account.
- Scopes: `recording:read:admin`, `recording:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>mc</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Query Metadata of Recording if an On-Premise Meeting Connector was used for the meeting.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>trash</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Query trash.</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>List recordings from trash.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Do not list recordings from the trash. The default value is <code>false</code>. If you set it to <code>true</code>, you can use the <code>trash_type</code> property to indicate the type of Cloud recording that you need to retrieve.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The start date in 'yyyy-mm-dd' UTC format for the date range for which you would like to retrieve recordings. The maximum range can be a month. If no value is provided for this field, the default will be current date. For example, if you make the API request on June 30, 2020, without providing the <code>from</code> and <code>to</code> parameters, by default the value of 'from' field will be 2020-06-30 and the value of the 'to' field will be 2020-07-01. <strong>Note</strong>: The "trash" files cannot be filtered by date range and thus, the "from" and "to" fields should not be used for trash files.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date in 'yyyy-mm-dd' 'yyyy-mm-dd' UTC format.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trash type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of Cloud recording that you would like to retrieve from the trash. The value can be one of the following:</p><p>* <code>meeting_recordings</code></p><p>List all meeting recordings from the trash.</p><p>* <code>recording_file</code></p><p>List all individual recording files from the trash.</p></td><td><p>meeting_recordings</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Remove A Channel Member

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:remove-achannel-member>`

A [channel](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548) can have one
or multiple members. Use this API to remove a member from a chat channel. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/chat/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}/members/{memberId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_channel:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: For an account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this
API can only be used on behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has Edit permission for Chat Channels.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the Channel from where you would like to remove a member. This can be retrieved from the <a href="https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/">List Channels API</a>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Member Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Email address of the member whom you would like to be remove from the channel.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the channel owner.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Remove A User Level Channel Member

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:remove-auser-level-channel-member>`

A [channel](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548) can have one
or multiple members. Use this API to remove a member from a chat channel. This operation makes an
HTTP DELETE request to the /chat/channels/{channelId}/members/{memberId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_channel:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note that this API only supports user-managed
[OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/).

## Report Cloud Recording

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-cloud-recording>`

Retrieve cloud recording usage report for a specified period. You can only get cloud recording
reports that are one day ealier than the current date and for the most recent period of 6 months.
The date gap between from and to dates should be smaller or equal to 30 days. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /report/cloud_recording endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date in 'yyyy-mm-dd' format. The date range defined by the "from" and "to" parameters should only be one month as the report includes only one month worth of data at once.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Report Daily

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-daily>`

Retrieve daily report to access the account-wide usage of Zoom services for each day in a given
month. It lists the number of new users, meetings, participants, and meeting minutes. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /report/daily endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>year</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Year for this report.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>month</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Month for this report.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Report Meeting Participants

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-meeting-participants>`

Use this API to return a report of a past meeting with two or more participants, including the host.
To return a report for past meeting with only one participant, use the
[List meeting participants](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/) API. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /report/meetings/{meetingId}/participants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

## Report Meetings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-meetings>`

Retrieve [a report](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0065639) on
past meetings and webinars for a specified time period. The time range for the report is limited to
a month and the month must fall within the past six months. Meetings and webinars are returned only
if they have two or more unique participants. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/report/users/{userId}/meetings endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date in 'yyyy-mm-dd' format. The date range defined by the "from" and "to" parameters should only be one month as the report includes only one month worth of data at once.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PAST</p></li><li><p>PAST_ONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting types:</p><p>* <code>past</code></p><p>Past meetings.</p><p>* <code>pastOne</code></p><p>Past one user meetings.</p></td><td><p>PAST</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Report Operation Logs

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-operation-logs>`

The [Operations Logs](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067251)
report allows you to audit admin and user activity, such as adding a new user, changing account
settings, and deleting recordings. Use this API to retrieve operation logs report for a specified
period of time. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /report/operationlogs endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date in 'yyyy-mm-dd' format. The date range defined by the "from" and "to" parameters should only be one month as the report includes only one month worth of data at once.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Category type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li><li><p>USER_SETTINGS</p></li><li><p>ACCOUNT</p></li><li><p>BILLING</p></li><li><p>IM</p></li><li><p>RECORDING</p></li><li><p>PHONE_CONTACTS</p></li><li><p>WEBINAR</p></li><li><p>SUB_ACCOUNT</p></li><li><p>ROLE</p></li><li><p>ZOOM_ROOMS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Filter your response by a category type to see reports for a specific category. The value for this field can be one of the following: <code>all</code>, <code>user</code>, <code>user_settings</code>, <code>account</code>, <code>billing</code>, <code>im</code>, <code>recording</code>, <code>phone_contacts</code>, <code>webinar</code>, <code>sub_account</code>, <code>role</code>, or <code>zoom_rooms</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Report Sign In Sign Out Activities

Retrieve a list of sign in and sign out activity logs
[report](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060623) of users under a
Zoom account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /report/activities endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

## Report Telephone

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-telephone>`

The [telephone report](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066623)
allows you to view who dialed into meetings via phone (Audio Conferencing or SIP Connected Audio)
and which number they dialed into and other details. Use this API to get a telephone report for a
specified period of time. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /report/telephone
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- ZOOM-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Report Users

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-users>`

A user is considered to be an active host during the month specified in the "from" and "to" range,
if the user has hosted at least one meeting during this period. If the user didnât host any meetings
during this period, the user is considered to be inactive. The Active Hosts report displays a list
of meetings, participants, and meeting minutes for a specific time range, up to one month. The month
should fall within the last six months. The Inactive Hosts report pulls a list of users who were not
active during a specific period of time. Use this API to retrieve an active or inactive host report
for a specified period of time. The time range for the report is limited to a month and the month
should fall under the past six months. You can specify the type of report and date range using the
query parameters. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /report/users endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ACTIVE</p></li><li><p>INACTIVE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Active or inactive hosts.</p><p>* <code>active</code></p><p>Active hosts.</p><p>* <code>inactive</code></p><p>Inactive hosts.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>from</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date in 'yyyy-mm-dd' format. The date range defined by the "from" and "to" parameters should only be one month as the report includes only one month worth of data at once.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>to</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Report Webinar Qa

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:report-webinar-qa>`

The Question & Answer (Q&A) feature for webinars allows attendees to ask questions during the
webinar and for the panelists, co-hosts, and host to answer their questions. Use this API to
retrieve a report on question and answers from past webinars. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /report/webinars/{webinarId}/qa endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with Webinar add-on enabled.
- Scopes: `report:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Heavy`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID or the webinar UUID. If a webinar ID is provided in the request instead of a UUID, the response will be for the latest webinar instance. If a UUID starts with "/" or contains "//" (example: "/ajXp112QmuoKj4854875==\"), you must double encode the UUID before making an API request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Role Members

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:role-members>`

to list all the members that are assigned a specific role. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /roles/{roleId}/members endpoint.

## Roles

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:roles>`

List [roles](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983) on your
account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /roles endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan. For setting the initial role, you must be the Account Owner.
  For subsequent role management, you must be the Account Owner or user with role management
  permissions.

## Search Company Contacts

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:search-company-contacts>`

A user under an organizationâs Zoom account has internal users listed under Company Contacts in the
Zoom Client. Use this API to search users that are in the company contacts of a Zoom account. Using
the `search_key` query parameter, provide either first name, last name, or the email address of the
user that you would like to search for. Optionally, set `query_presence_status` to `true` to include
the presence status of a contact. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /contacts
endpoint.

- Scopes: `contact:read:admin`, `contact:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provide the keyword - either first name, last name or email of the contact whom you have to search for.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query presence status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set <code>query_presence_status</code> to <code>true</code> to include the presence status of a contact in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of records to be returned with a single API call.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Senda Chat Message

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:senda-chat-message>`

Send chat messages on Zoom to either an individual user who is in your contact list or to a
[channel](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063548) of which you
are a member. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. To send a message to a contact, provide the contactâs email address in the
`to_contact` field. To send a message to a channel, provide the channelâs ID in `to_channel`
parameter. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /chat/users/{userId}/messages endpoint.

- Scopes: `chat_message:write`, `chat_message:write:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

Note: For an account-level [OAuth app](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/create/), this
API can only be used on behalf of a user who is assigned with a
[role](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064983#:~:text=Each%20user%20in%20a%20Zoom,owner%2C%20administrator%2C%20or%20member.&text=Role%2Dbased%20access%20control%20enables,needs%20to%20view%20or%20edit)
that has the Edit permission for Chat Messages.

## Sendimmessages

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:sendimmessages>`

Send chat message to a user. Note that this API only supports OAuth 2.0. This operation makes an
HTTP POST request to the /im/users/me/chat/messages endpoint.

- Scope: `imchat:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## Set Up Account

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:set-up-account>`

After assigning a Zoom phone license to an account, an admin or account owner can proceed with the
[initial Zoom phone set up](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060257)
using this API. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /accounts/{accountId}/phone/setup
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Paid account. A Pro or a higher account plan. Master account option enabled.
- Scopes: `phone:write:admin`, `phone:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## Switch User Account

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:switch-user-account>`

Disassociate a user from one account and move the user to another account under the same master
account. With this API, a user under a master account or a sub account can be moved to another sub
account within the same master account. To move a user from a master account to a sub account, use
`me` as the value for `accountId`. In this scenario, "me" refers to the Account ID of the master
account. To move a user from one sub account to another sub account, provide the sub accountâs
Account ID as the value for `accountId`. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/accounts/{accountId}/users/{userId}/account endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account should have Pro or a higher plan with master account option enabled.
  The user whose account needs to be switched should not be an admin or an owner of that account.
  The user should not have the same
  [managed domain](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066259) as the
  account owner.
- Scope: `user:master`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Account ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Testget Record Archived Files

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:testget-record-archived-files>`

List the archived recording files of the specific meeting instance. For more information, read our
[Managing archiving of meeting and webinar data](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067188)
documentation. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/past_meetings/{meetingUUID}/archive_files endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Enable cloud recording. Follow the
  [enablement process](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067188#h_01ENPBD3WR68D7FAKTBY92SG45)
  to access the archiving feature.
- Scopes: `recording:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Trackingfield Get

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:trackingfield-get>`

[Tracking fields](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067020) enable
you to analyze usage by various fields within an organization. When scheduling a meeting, the
tracking field is included in the meeting options. Use this API to get information on a tracking
field. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /tracking_fields/{fieldId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, Education, API, or higher plan
- Scopes: `trackingfield:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Trackingfield List

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:trackingfield-list>`

[Tracking fields](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0067020) enable
you to analyze usage by various fields within an organization. Use this API to list all the tracking
fields on your Zoom account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /tracking_fields
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, Education, API, or higher plan
- Scopes: `trackingfield:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
