## User

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user>`

View a specific userâs information on a Zoom account. A Zoom account can have one or more users. For
user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Note: Users who have not activated their account have a status of pending, and
the created_at time displays the time at which the API call was made. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /users/{userId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:read:admin`, `user:read`, `user_info:read`. Note: The `user_info:read` scope is only
  available when you pass the `me` value for the `$userId` value.
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. If you do not query this parameter, this value defaults to null (<code>false</code>).</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## User Assistant Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-assistant-create>`

Use this API to assign assistants to a user. In the request body, provide either the userâs ID or
the userâs email address. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Assistants are the users to whom the current user has assigned
[the scheduling privilege](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061749).
These assistants can schedule meeting on behalf of the current user as well as manage and act as an
alternative host for all meetings if the admin has enabled
[Co-host option](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066640) on the
account. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /users/{userId}/assistants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The user as well as the assistant must have Licensed or an On-prem license.
  Assistants must be under the current userâs account.
- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. ## User Assistant Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-assistant-delete>`

Delete a specific assistant of a user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Assistants are the users to whom the current user has assigned
[the scheduling privilege](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061749).
These assistants can schedule meeting on behalf of the current user as well as manage and act as an
alternative host for all meetings if the admin has enabled
[Co-host option](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066640) on the
account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /users/{userId}/assistants/{assistantId}
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The user as well as the assistant must have Licensed or an On-prem license.
  Assistants must be under the current userâs account.
- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## User Assistants

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-assistants>`

[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Assistants are the users to whom the current user has assigned
[the scheduling privilege](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061749).
These assistants can schedule meeting on behalf of the current user as well as manage and act as an
alternative host for all meetings if the admin has enabled
[Co-host option](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066640) on the
account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{userId}/assistants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Current user as well as the assistant must have Licensed or an On-prem license.
  Assistants must be under the current userâs account.
- Scopes: `user:read:admin`, `user:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## User Assistants Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-assistants-delete>`

Delete all assistants of the current user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Assistants are the users to whom the current user has assigned
[the scheduling privilege](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061749).
These assistants can schedule meeting on behalf of the current user as well as manage and act as an
alternative host for all meetings if the admin has enabled
[Co-host option](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066640) on the
account. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /users/{userId}/assistants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The user as well as the assistant must have Licensed or an On-prem license.
  Assistants must be under the current userâs account.
- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## User Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-create>`

A Zoom account can have one or more users. Use this API to add a new user to your account. This
operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /users endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan
- Scopes: `user:write:admin` `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## User Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-delete>`

Use this API to disassociate (unlink) a user or permanently delete a user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Deleting a user permanently removes the user and their data from Zoom. Users can
create a new Zoom account using the same email address. An account owner or an account admin can
transfer meetings, webinars, and cloud recordings to another Zoom user account before deleting.
Disassociating a user unlinks the user from the associated Zoom account and provides the user their
own basic free Zoom account. The disassociated user can then purchase their own Zoom licenses. An
account owner or account admin can transfer the userâs meetings, webinars, and cloud recordings to
another user before disassociation. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/users/{userId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DISASSOCIATE</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Delete action options:</p><p>* <code>disassociate</code></p><p>Disassociate a user.</p><p>* <code>delete</code></p><p>Permanently delete a user. Note: To delete pending user in the account, use <code>disassociate</code>.</p></td><td><p>DISASSOCIATE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Transfer email.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer meeting</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Transfer meeting.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer webinar</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Transfer webinar.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer recording</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Transfer recording.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## User Email

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-email>`

Verify if a userâs email is registered with Zoom. Note: You can successfully check if a user is a
registered Zoom user only if the user signed up for Zoom via email and is within your account. If
you provide an email address of a user who is not in your account, the value of "existed_email"
parameter will be "false" irrespective of whether or not the user is registered with Zoom. The
response of this API call will not include users who joined Zoom using options such as "Sign in with
SSO", "Sign in with Google", or "Sign in with Facebook" even if they are in the same account as
yours. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/email endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:read:admin` `user:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## User Email Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-email-update>`

Change a userâs
[email address](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0063624) on a Zoom
account that has managed domain set up. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. If the Zoom account in which the user belongs has multiple
[managed domains](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066259), then
the email to be updated must match one of the managed domains. A userâs email address can only be
changed for a maximum of 3 times in a day (24 hours). This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to
the /users/{userId}/email endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Managed domain must be enabled in the account. The new email address should not
  already exist in Zoom.
- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## User Pa Cs

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-pacs>`

Use this API to list a userâs
[Personal Audio Conference](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060449)
accounts. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. PAC allows Pro or higher account holders to host meetings through PSTN (phone
dial-in) only. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{userId}/pac endpoint.

- Prerequisites: A Pro or higher plan with
  [Premium Audio Conferencing](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060449)
  add-on. Personal Audio Conference must be enabled in the userâs profile.
- Scopes: `pac:read:admin`, `pac:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. ## User Password

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-password>`

Update a userâs
[password](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066204) for logging
into Zoom. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. After this request is processed successfully, an email notification is sent to
the user stating that the password was changed. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/users/{userId}/password endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Owner or admin of the Zoom account.
- Scopes: `user:write:admin` `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## User Permission

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-permission>`

Use this API to get permissions that have been granted to the user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Users can be assigned a set of permissions that allows them to access only the
pages/information that a user needs to view or edit. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/users/{userId}/permissions endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:read:admin`, `user:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## User Picture

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-picture>`

Upload a userâs profile picture. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Provide `multipart/form-data` as the value of the `content-type` header for this
request. This API supports `.jpeg` and `.png` file formats. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /users/{userId}/picture endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## User Scheduler Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-scheduler-delete>`

Delete a scheduler. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Schedulers are users on whose behalf the current user (assistant) can schedule
meetings for. By calling this API, the current user will no longer be a scheduling assistant of this
scheduler. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/users/{userId}/schedulers/{schedulerId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Current user must be under the same account as the scheduler.
- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## User Schedulers

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-schedulers>`

List all the schedulers of a user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Schedulers in this context are the users for whom the current user can schedule
meetings for. For example, if the current user (the user whose `userId` was passed in the `path`
parameter) is "user A", the response of this API will contain a list of user(s), for whom user A can
schedule and manage meetings. User A is the assistant of these users and thus has scheduling
privilege for these user(s). This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/users/{userId}/schedulers endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Current user must be under the same account as the scheduler.
- Scopes: `user:read:admin`, `user:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## User Settings

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-settings>`

Retrieve a userâs settings. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{userId}/settings
endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:read:admin`, `user:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. Facebook OAuth</p><p>* <code>1</code> - Google OAuth</p><p>* <code>24</code> - Apple OAuth</p><p>* <code>27</code> - Microsoft OAuth</p><p>* <code>97</code> - Mobile device</p><p>* <code>98</code> - RingCentral OAuth</p><p>* <code>99</code>- API user</p><p>* <code>100</code> - Zoom Work email</p><p>* <code>101</code> - Single Sign-On (SSO)</p><p>The following login methods are available only in China:</p><p>* <code>11</code> - Phone number</p><p>* <code>21</code> - WeChat</p><p>* <code>23</code> - Alipay</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>option</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MEETING_AUTHENTICATION</p></li><li><p>RECORDING_AUTHENTICATION</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>* <code>meeting_authentication</code></p><p>Use this query parameter to view <a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0061263">meeting authentication configuration</a> applied on the userâs account.</p><p>* <code>recording_authentication</code></p><p>Use this query parameter to view <a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0067223">recording authentication configuration</a> applied on the userâs account.</p><p>* <code>meeting_security</code></p><p>Use this query parameter to view meeting security settings applied on the userâs account.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom query fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provide the name of the field by which you would like to filter the response. For example, if you provide "host_video" as the value of this field, you will get a response similar to the following: { "schedule_meeting": { "host_video": false } }. You can provide multiple values by separating them with commas (example: "host_video,participant_video).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## User Settings Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-settings-update>`

Update a userâs settings. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /users/{userId}/settings
endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## User Sso Token Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-ssotoken-delete>`

Revoke a userâs SSO token. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. After calling this API, the SSO user will be logged out of their current Zoom
session. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /users/{userId}/token endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

## User Status

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-status>`

Use this API to
[deactivate](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066997) an active
user or to [reactivate](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066997) a
deactivated user. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. An account owner or admins can deactivate as well as activate a user in a Zoom
account. Deactivating a user will remove all licenses associated with a user. It will prevent the
deactivated user from logging into their Zoom account. A deactivated user can be reactivated.
Reactivating a user grants the user access to login to their Zoom account. This operation makes an
HTTP PUT request to the /users/{userId}/status endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:write:admin`, `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## User Token

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-token>`

Retrieve a userâs token. For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This token is used for starting meetings with the Client SDK. If a user signed
into Zoom using Google or Facebook, a null value will be returned for the token. To get the token
with this API, ask the user to sign into Zoom using their email and password instead. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{userId}/token endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:read:admin`, `user:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TOKEN</p></li><li><p>ZAK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>User token types:</p><p>* <code>token</code></p><p>Used for starting meetings with the client SDK. This token expires in 14 days and a new token will be returned after the expiry.</p><p>* <code>zak</code></p><p>Used for generating the start meeting URL. The token expiration time is two hours. For API users, the expiration time is 90 days.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>ttl</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use this field in conjunction with the <code>type</code> field where the value of <code>type</code> field is <code>zak</code>. The value of this field denotes the expiry time of the <code>zak</code> token in seconds. For example, if you would like the zak token to be expired after one hour of the token generation, the value of this field should be <code>3600</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## User Tsp

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-tsp>`

Each user can have a maximum of two TSP accounts. Use this API to retrieve details of a specific TSP
account enabled for a specific user. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/users/{userId}/tsp/{tspId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `tsp:read:admin` `tsp:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. ## User Tsp Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-tspupdate>`

Update a userâs TSP account. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/users/{userId}/tsp/{tspId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `tsp:write:admin` `tsp:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## User Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-update>`

Update information on a userâs
[Zoom profile](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0060639). For
user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /users/{userId} endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:write:admin` `user:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## User Vanity Name

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-vanity-name>`

A personal meeting room is a virtual meeting room that can be permanently assigned to a user. Use
this API to check if a personal meeting room with the given name exists or not. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /users/vanity_name endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:read:admin` `user:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## User Zak

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:user-zak>`

Get a userâs Zoom Access Token (ZAK). You can use a ZAK to enable a non-login user to join a meeting
on your app. Non-login users do not need to enter their username and password to join meetings. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/me/zak endpoint.

- Scope: `user_zak:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Users

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:users>`

A Zoom account can have one or more users. Use this API to list users on your account. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users endpoint.

- Scopes: `user:read:admin`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ACTIVE</p></li><li><p>INACTIVE</p></li><li><p>PENDING</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>User statuses:</p><p>* <code>active</code></p><p>Users with an active status.</p><p>* <code>inactive</code></p><p>Users who are deactivated.</p><p>* <code>pending</code></p><p>Users with a pending status.</p></td><td><p>ACTIVE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Role ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of the role. ## Webinar

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar>`

Zoom users with a Webinar Plan have access to creating and managing Webinars. Webinar allows a host
to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 10,000 attendees. Use this API to get details of a scheduled
webinar. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /webinars/{webinarId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin` `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID in the <code>long</code> format, which is represented as the int64 data type in JSON.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Occurrence ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique Identifier that identifies an occurrence of a recurring webinar. <a href="https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&amp;sysparm_article=KB0062461">Recurring webinars</a> can have a maximum of 50 occurrences. When you create a recurring Webinar using <a href="https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/">Create a Webinar API</a>, you can retrieve the Occurrence ID from the response of the API call.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show previous occurrences</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set the value of this field to <code>true</code> if you would like to view Webinar details of all previous occurrences of a recurring Webinar.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Webinar Absentees

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-absentees>`

/past_webinars/{WebinarUUID}/absentees endpoint.

- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin` `webinar:read`

## Webinar Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-create>`

Use this API to schedule a webinar for a user (host). For user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Zoom users with a Webinar Plan have access to creating and managing webinars.
Webinars allow a host to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 10,000 attendees. This operation makes an
HTTP POST request to the /users/{userId}/webinars endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin`, `webinar:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. ## Webinar Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-delete>`

Delete a webinar. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /webinars/{webinarId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin` `webinar:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID in the <code>long</code> format, which is represented as the int64 data type in JSON.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Occurrence ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting occurrence ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cancel webinar reminder</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p><code>true</code>: Notify panelists and registrants about the webinar cancellation via email. <code>false</code>: Do not send any email notification to webinar registrants and panelists. The default value of this field is <code>false</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Webinar Invite Links Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-invite-links-create>`

Use this API to create a batch of invitation links for a webinar. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/invite_links endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Business, Education, or API Plan with Webinar add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin`, `webinar:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Webinar Live Stream Status Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-live-stream-status-update>`

Zoom allows users to
[live stream a webinar](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064210)
to a custom platform. Use this API to update the status of a webinarâs live stream. This operation
makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/livestream/status endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on. Live streaming details must have been
  [configured](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0064210#h_01589a6f-a40a-4e18-a448-cb746e52ebc5)
  for the webinar.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin` `webinar:write`

## Webinar Panelist Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-panelist-create>`

Panelists in a webinar can view and send video, screen share, annotate, etc., and do much more
compared to attendees in a webinar. Use this API to
[add panelists](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066116) to a
scheduled webinar. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/panelists
endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin` `webinar:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID in the <code>long</code> format, which is represented as the int64 data type in JSON.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Webinar Panelist Delete

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-panelist-delete>`

[Remove](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0066116) a single
panelist from a webinar. You can retrieve the `panelistId` by calling **List Panelists API**. This
operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/panelists/{panelistId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin` `webinar:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID in the <code>long</code> format, which is represented as the int64 data type in JSON.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Panelist Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The panelist ID or panelist email.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Webinar Panelists

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-panelists>`

Panelists in a webinar can view and send video, screen share, annotate, etc., and do much more
compared to attendees in a webinar. Use this API to list all the panelists of a webinar. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/panelists endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or a higher plan with Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin` `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

## Webinar Registrant Create

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-registrant-create>`

Zoom users with a Webinar Plan have access to creating and managing webinars. Webinar allows a host
to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 10,000 attendees. Scheduling a
[Webinar with registration](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061631)
requires your registrants to complete a brief form before receiving the link to join the Webinar.
Use this API to create and submit the registration of a user for a webinar. This operation makes an
HTTP POST request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/registrants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin` `webinar:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID in the <code>long</code> format, which is represented as the int64 data type in JSON.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Occurrence ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Occurrence ID. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Webinar Registrant Get

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-registrant-get>`

Zoom users with a Webinar Plan have access to creating and managing Webinars. Webinar allows a host
to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 10,000 attendees. Scheduling a
[Webinar with registration](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061631)
requires your registrants to complete a brief form before receiving the link to join the Webinar.
Use this API to get details on a specific user who has registered for the Webinar. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/registrants/{registrantId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: The account must have a Webinar plan.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin` `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

requires your registrants to complete a brief form with fields and questions before they can receive
the link to join the webinar. Use this API to update registration questions and fields of a

  should be set as required to use this API.

## Webinar Registrants

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-registrants>`

Zoom users with a Webinar Plan have access to creating and managing webinars. Webinar allows a host
to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 10,000 attendees. Scheduling a
[Webinar with registration](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061631)
requires your registrants to complete a brief form before receiving the link to join the Webinar.
Use this API to list all the users that have registered for a webinar. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /webinars/{webinarId}/registrants endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin` `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webinar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The webinar ID in the <code>long</code> format, which is represented as the int64 data type in JSON.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Occurrence ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The meeting occurrence ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PENDING</p></li><li><p>APPROVED</p></li><li><p>DENIED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The registrant status: <code>pending</code> - Registrantâs status is pending. <code>approved</code> - Registrantâs status is approved. <code>denied</code> - Registrantâs status is denied.</p></td><td><p>APPROVED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Tracking source ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The tracking source ID for the registrants. ## Webinar Registrants Questions Get

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-registrants-questions-get>`

Scheduling a
[Webinar with registration](https://support.zoom.com/hc/en/article?id=zm_kb&sysparm_article=KB0061631)
requires your registrants to complete a brief form with fields and questions before they can receive
the link to join the Webinar. Use this API to list registration questions and fields that are to be
answered by users while registering for a webinar. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/webinars/{webinarId}/registrants/questions endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin` `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Webinar Update

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinar-update>`

Zoom users with a Webinar Plan have access to creating and managing Webinars. Webinar allows a host
to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 10,000 attendees. Use this API to make updates to a scheduled
webinar. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the /webinars/{webinarId} endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:write:admin` `webinar:write`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Light`

## Webinars

`<zoom-connector-mule-4:webinars>`

Use this API to list all the webinars that are scheduled by or on behalf a user (webinar host). For
user-level apps, pass
[the `me` value](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/using-zoom-apis/#the-me-keyword) instead of the
`userId` parameter. Zoom users with a Webinar Plan have access to creating and managing webinars.
Webinars allow a host to broadcast a Zoom meeting to up to 10,000 attendees. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /users/{userId}/webinars endpoint.

- Prerequisites: Pro or higher plan with a Webinar Add-on.
- Scopes: `webinar:read:admin`, `webinar:read`
- [Rate Limit Label](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rate-limits/): `Medium`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The user ID or email address of the user. For user-level apps, pass <code>me</code> as the value for userId.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records returned within a single API call.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output
